User registers with name and email 14→2. User logs in with email 15→3. 6-digit confirmation code sent to email 16→4. User enters code to complete authentication 17→5. Server returns JWT token 18→6. Token stored in localStorage and global state 19→ 20→**Validation:** 21→ 22→- **Email:** Required field (no format validation on frontend) 23→- **Name:** Required field for registration 24→- **Auth Code:** 25→ - Exactly 6 digits 26→ - Only numeric characters 27→ - Pattern: `/^\d*$/` 28→ - Min/max length: 6 29→ 30→**Implementation:** 31→ 32→```typescript 33→ 41→ 42→ 43→``` 44→ 45→**Session Management:** 46→ 47→- Token stored in `localStorage` under `ACCESS_TOKEN` key 48→- Token loaded on app initialization via `authService.initialize()` 49→- Token cleared on logout with `authService.logout()` 50→- Token sent with all API requests via Axios interceptor 51→- React Query cache cleared on logout 52→ 53→--- 54→ 55→## Validation Rules 56→ 57→### General Validation Patterns 58→ 59→**Location:** `src/shared/constants/validationPatterns.ts` 60→ 61→#### NUMBER Pattern 62→ 63→```typescript 64→{ 65→ value: /^\d*$/, 66→ message: 'Only numbers are allowed' 67→} 68→``` 69→ 70→**Usage:** Auth codes, quantities, numeric-only fields 71→ 72→--- 73→ 74→#### PRICE Pattern 75→ 76→```typescript 77→{ 78→ value: /^-?(\d{1,3}(?:\s?\d{3})*)?(?:[.,]\d{0,2})?$/, 79→ message: 'Only valid prices are allowed (e.g., 123, 123.45)' 80→} 81→``` 82→ 83→**Features:** 84→ 85→- Supports negative numbers (`-123.45`) 86→- Supports thousands separators with space (`1 000`) 87→- Supports decimal with comma or period (`123,45` or `123.45`) 88→- Supports up to 2 decimal places 89→- Optional whole number part 90→ 91→**Usage:** Transaction amounts, budget amounts, monetary values 92→ 93→--- 94→ 95→### Replace Patterns 96→ 97→**Location:** `src/shared/constants/replacePatterns.ts` 98→ 99→#### PRICE Replace Pattern 100→ 101→```typescript 102→{ 103→ pattern: /,/, 104→ replacement: '.' 105→} 106→``` 107→ 108→**Purpose:** Normalizes comma decimals to period decimals for consistent parsing 109→ 110→**Implementation:** 111→ 112→```typescript 113→ 119→ 120→ 121→``` 122→ 123→**Behavior:** 124→ 125→- User types: `123,45` 126→- Stored as: `123.45` 127→- Enables `parseFloat()` to work correctly 128→ 129→--- 130→ 131→### Form Field Validation 132→ 133→**Rules Applied by AuField:** 134→ 135→1. **Required Fields:** 136→ 137→ ```typescript 138→ 139→ ``` 140→ 141→ - Error: "This field is required" 142→ 143→2. **Min Length:** 144→ 145→ ```typescript 146→ 147→ ``` 148→ 149→ - Error: "Minimum length — 6 characters" 150→ 151→3. **Max Length:** 152→ 153→ ```typescript 154→ 155→ ``` 156→ 157→ - Error: "Maximum length — 6 characters" 158→ 159→4. **Pattern:** 160→ 161→ ```typescript 162→ 163→ ``` 164→ 165→ - Error: Custom pattern message 166→ - Blocks invalid input in real-time 167→ 168→5. **Clear Button:** 169→ ```typescript 170→ 171→ ``` 172→ - Shows clear button when value differs from `clearValue` 173→ - Resets field to `clearValue` on click 174→ 175→--- 176→ 177→## Transaction Rules 178→ 179→### Amount Handling 180→ 181→**Location:** `src/pages/transactions/form/TransactionForm.tsx:64-72` 182→ 183→**Rule:** Transaction amounts are normalized based on category type. 184→ 185→#### For EXPENSE Transactions 186→ 187→```typescript 188→if (categoryType === CategoryTypeE.EXPENSE && amount > 0) { 189→ amount = -amount; // Negate positive amounts 190→} 191→``` 192→ 193→**Example:** 194→ 195→- User enters: `50.00` 196→- Category: EXPENSE 197→- Stored as: `-50.00` 198→ 199→--- 200→ 201→#### For INCOME Transactions 202→ 203→```typescript 204→if (categoryType === CategoryTypeE.INCOME && amount < 0) { 205→ amount = Math.abs(amount); // Make negative amounts positive 206→} 207→``` 208→ 209→**Example:** 210→ 211→- User enters: `-100.00` 212→- Category: INCOME 213→- Stored as: `100.00` 214→ 215→--- 216→ 217→### Transaction Type Determination 218→ 219→**Location:** `src/pages/transactions/form/TransactionForm.tsx:85-88` 220→ 221→**Rule:** Transaction type is determined by the sign of the amount (before category normalization). 222→ 223→```typescript 224→type: parseInt(data.amount, 10) > 0 225→ ? TransactionTypeE.DEPOSIT 226→ : TransactionTypeE.WITHDRAWAL; 227→``` 228→ 229→**Types:** 230→ 231→- `DEPOSIT`: Amount is positive 232→- `WITHDRAWAL`: Amount is negative or zero 233→ 234→--- 235→ 236→### Manual Transaction Defaults 237→ 238→**Location:** `src/pages/transactions/form/TransactionForm.tsx:91-94` 239→ 240→**Rule:** Manually created transactions have fixed properties: 241→ 242→```typescript 243→{ 244→ source: TransactionSourceE.MANUAL, 245→ pending: false, 246→ confirmed: true, 247→} 248→``` 249→ 250→**Properties:** 251→ 252→- `source`: Always `MANUAL` (vs. imported from bank) 253→- `pending`: Always `false` (manual transactions are finalized) 254→- `confirmed`: Always `true` (user explicitly created it) 255→ 256→--- 257→ 258→### Transaction Date Constraints 259→ 260→**Location:** `src/pages/transactions/form/TransactionForm.tsx:42, 186-189` 261→ 262→**Rule:** Transaction date cannot be in the future. 263→ 264→```typescript 265→const currentDate = new Date(); 266→ 267→ 271→``` 272→ 273→**Behavior:** 274→ 275→- User can select today or any past date 276→- Future dates are disabled in date picker 277→ 278→--- 279→ 280→### Required Transaction Fields 281→ 282→**Fields:** 283→ 284→- Transaction name (string) 285→- Bank account (UUID, single selection) 286→- Amount (decimal, validated with PRICE pattern) 287→- Currency (enum, single selection) 288→- Transaction date (date, max: today) 289→- Category type (INCOME or EXPENSE) 290→- Categories (array of category UUIDs) 291→ 292→**Optional Fields:** 293→ 294→- Merchant name (string) 295→- Notes (textarea) 296→ 297→--- 298→ 299→## Budget Rules 300→ 301→### Budget Hierarchy 302→ 303→**Structure:** 304→ 305→``` 306→Budget 307→ └── Budget Groups 308→ └── Budget Items 309→``` 310→ 311→**Relations:** 312→ 313→- One budget has many groups 314→- One group has many items 315→- Items belong to exactly one group 316→- Groups belong to exactly one budget 317→ 318→--- 319→ 320→### Budget Calculations 321→ 322→**Location:** `src/pages/budget/group/BudgetGroup.tsx:33-55` 323→ 324→**Rule:** Group totals are calculated by aggregating all items in the group. 325→ 326→#### Income vs. Expense Handling 327→ 328→**Key Rule:** INCOME items **subtract** from totals, EXPENSE items **add** to totals. 329→ 330→**Rationale:** From a budget planning perspective: 331→ 332→- Expenses increase your budget need (positive) 333→- Income decreases your budget need (negative/offset) 334→ 335→--- 336→ 337→#### Total Planned Amount 338→ 339→```typescript 340→const totalPlaned = budgetItems.reduce((res, currentValue) => { 341→ if (currentValue.type === CategoryTypeE.INCOME) { 342→ return res - currentValue.plannedAmount; // Subtract income 343→ } 344→ return res + currentValue.plannedAmount; // Add expense 345→}, 0); 346→``` 347→ 348→**Example:** 349→ 350→- Item 1 (EXPENSE): $500 planned 351→- Item 2 (INCOME): $200 planned 352→- Item 3 (EXPENSE): $300 planned 353→- **Total:** $500 - $200 + $300 = **$600** 354→ 355→--- 356→ 357→#### Total Actual Amount 358→ 359→```typescript 360→const totalActual = budgetItems.reduce((res, currentValue) => { 361→ if (currentValue.type === CategoryTypeE.INCOME) { 362→ return res - currentValue.actualAmount; // Subtract income 363→ } 364→ return res + currentValue.actualAmount; // Add expense 365→}, 0); 366→``` 367→ 368→**Tracks actual spending/income against budget.** 369→ 370→--- 371→ 372→#### Total Remaining Amount 373→ 374→```typescript 375→const totalRemaining = budgetItems.reduce((res, currentValue) => { 376→ if (currentValue.type === CategoryTypeE.INCOME) { 377→ return res - currentValue.remainingAmount; // Subtract income 378→ } 379→ return res + currentValue.remainingAmount; // Add expense 380→}, 0); 381→``` 382→ 383→**Calculated as:** `remaining = planned - actual` 384→ 385→--- 386→ 387→### Budget Item Amounts 388→ 389→**Three amount types tracked per item:** 390→ 391→1. **Planned Amount:** Budgeted/target amount 392→2. **Actual Amount:** Real transactions total 393→3. **Remaining Amount:** Difference (planned - actual) 394→ 395→**Calculation (server-side):** 396→ 397→``` 398→remaining = planned - actual 399→``` 400→ 401→**Display:** 402→ 403→- Positive remaining: Under budget ✅ 404→- Negative remaining: Over budget ❌ 405→- Zero remaining: Exactly on budget 406→ 407→--- 408→ 409→### Budget Item Display Rules 410→ 411→**Location:** `src/pages/budget/item/BudgetItem.tsx:62, 84` 412→ 413→**Rule:** Display logic differs for INCOME vs. EXPENSE items. 414→ 415→```typescript 416→{budgetItem.type === CategoryTypeE.INCOME ? ( 417→ 418→) : ( 419→ 420→)} 421→``` 422→ 423→**Income Items:** 424→ 425→- Green/positive styling 426→- Inverted display (income reduces budget need) 427→ 428→**Expense Items:** 429→ 430→- Red/negative styling 431→- Normal display (expenses increase budget need) 432→ 433→--- 434→ 435→## Category Rules 436→ 437→### Category Types 438→ 439→**Location:** `src/shared/types/CategoryTypeE.ts` 440→ 441→**Types:** 442→ 443→```typescript 444→export enum CategoryTypeE { 445→ INCOME = 'income', 446→ EXPENSE = 'expense', 447→} 448→``` 449→ 450→**Rules:** 451→ 452→- Every category must have a type 453→- Type determines amount sign convention 454→- Type affects budget calculations 455→- Type affects transaction amount normalization 456→ 457→--- 458→ 459→### User-Defined Categories 460→ 461→**Location:** `src/pages/userCatigory/` 462→ 463→**Rules:** 464→ 465→- Users can create custom categories 466→- Each category has a name, type, and optional color 467→- Categories are reusable across transactions and budgets 468→- Default category type: `INCOME` 469→ 470→**Default Form Values:** 471→ 472→```typescript 473→{ 474→ type: [CategoryTypeE.INCOME], // Default to income 475→} 476→``` 477→ 478→--- 479→ 480→### Category Assignment 481→ 482→**Transactions:** 483→ 484→- Multiple categories can be assigned to one transaction 485→- Field name: `categories` (array of UUIDs) 486→- Required for transaction creation 487→ 488→**Budget Items:** 489→ 490→- Each budget item links to one or more categories 491→- Tracks spending per category 492→ 493→--- 494→ 495→## Validation Error Handling 496→ 497→### Field-Level Errors 498→ 499→**Pattern:** 500→ 501→```typescript 502→import { useController, useFormContext } from 'react-hook-form'; 503→ 504→const { field, fieldState: { error } } = useController({ 505→ name, 506→ control, 507→ rules: { 508→ required: 'This field is required', 509→ maxLength: { value: 100, message: 'Too long' }, 510→ }, 511→}); 512→ 513→// Display error 514→{error &&

{error.message}

} 515→``` 516→ 517→**Error Display:** 518→ 519→- Errors shown below input field 520→- Red text and border styling 521→- Cleared on user input 522→- Prevents form submission until resolved 523→ 524→--- # Business Rules Documentation Business logic, validation rules, and constraints that govern the application. ## Authentication Rules ### Email-Based Authentication **Location:** `src/pages/auth/AuthPage.tsx`, `src/pages/login/LoginPage.tsx`, `src/pages/registration/RegistrationPage.tsx` **Flow:** 1. User registers with name and email 2. User logs in with email 3. 6-digit confirmation code sent to email 4. User enters code to complete authentication 5. Server returns JWT token 6. Token stored in localStorage and global state **Validation:** - **Email:** Required field (no format validation on frontend) - **Name:** Required field for registration - **Auth Code:** - Exactly 6 digits - Only numeric characters - Pattern: `/^\d*$/` - Min/max length: 6 **Implementation:** ```typescript ``` **Session Management:** - Token stored in `localStorage` under `ACCESS_TOKEN` key - Token loaded on app initialization via `authService.initialize()` - Token cleared on logout with `authService.logout()` - Token sent with all API requests via Axios interceptor - React Query cache cleared on logout --- ## Validation Rules ### General Validation Patterns **Location:** `src/shared/constants/validationPatterns.ts` #### NUMBER Pattern ```typescript { value: /^\d*$/, message: 'Only numbers are allowed' } ``` **Usage:** Auth codes, quantities, numeric-only fields --- #### PRICE Pattern ```typescript { value: /^-?(\d{1,3}(?:\s?\d{3})*)?(?:[.,]\d{0,2})?$/, message: 'Only valid prices are allowed (e.g., 123, 123.45)' } ``` **Features:** - Supports negative numbers (`-123.45`) - Supports thousands separators with space (`1 000`) - Supports decimal with comma or period (`123,45` or `123.45`) - Supports up to 2 decimal places - Optional whole number part **Usage:** Transaction amounts, budget amounts, monetary values --- ### Replace Patterns **Location:** `src/shared/constants/replacePatterns.ts` #### PRICE Replace Pattern ```typescript { pattern: /,/, replacement: '.' } ``` **Purpose:** Normalizes comma decimals to period decimals for consistent parsing **Implementation:** ```typescript ``` **Behavior:** - User types: `123,45` - Stored as: `123.45` - Enables `parseFloat()` to work correctly --- ### Form Field Validation **Rules Applied by AuField:** 1. **Required Fields:** ```typescript ``` - Error: "This field is required" 2. **Min Length:** ```typescript ``` - Error: "Minimum length — 6 characters" 3. **Max Length:** ```typescript ``` - Error: "Maximum length — 6 characters" 4. **Pattern:** ```typescript ``` - Error: Custom pattern message - Blocks invalid input in real-time 5. **Clear Button:** ```typescript ``` - Shows clear button when value differs from `clearValue` - Resets field to `clearValue` on click --- ## Transaction Rules ### Amount Handling **Location:** `src/pages/transactions/form/TransactionForm.tsx:64-72` **Rule:** Transaction amounts are normalized based on category type. #### For EXPENSE Transactions ```typescript if (categoryType === CategoryTypeE.EXPENSE && amount > 0) { amount = -amount; // Negate positive amounts } ``` **Example:** - User enters: `50.00` - Category: EXPENSE - Stored as: `-50.00` --- #### For INCOME Transactions ```typescript if (categoryType === CategoryTypeE.INCOME && amount < 0) { amount = Math.abs(amount); // Make negative amounts positive } ``` **Example:** - User enters: `-100.00` - Category: INCOME - Stored as: `100.00` --- ### Transaction Type Determination **Location:** `src/pages/transactions/form/TransactionForm.tsx:85-88` **Rule:** Transaction type is determined by the sign of the amount (before category normalization). ```typescript type: parseInt(data.amount, 10) > 0 ? TransactionTypeE.DEPOSIT : TransactionTypeE.WITHDRAWAL; ``` **Types:** - `DEPOSIT`: Amount is positive - `WITHDRAWAL`: Amount is negative or zero --- ### Manual Transaction Defaults **Location:** `src/pages/transactions/form/TransactionForm.tsx:91-94` **Rule:** Manually created transactions have fixed properties: ```typescript { source: TransactionSourceE.MANUAL, pending: false, confirmed: true, } ``` **Properties:** - `source`: Always `MANUAL` (vs. imported from bank) - `pending`: Always `false` (manual transactions are finalized) - `confirmed`: Always `true` (user explicitly created it) --- ### Transaction Date Constraints **Location:** `src/pages/transactions/form/TransactionForm.tsx:42, 186-189` **Rule:** Transaction date cannot be in the future. ```typescript const currentDate = new Date(); ``` **Behavior:** - User can select today or any past date - Future dates are disabled in date picker --- ### Required Transaction Fields **Fields:** - Transaction name (string) - Bank account (UUID, single selection) - Amount (decimal, validated with PRICE pattern) - Currency (enum, single selection) - Transaction date (date, max: today) - Category type (INCOME or EXPENSE) - Categories (array of category UUIDs) **Optional Fields:** - Merchant name (string) - Notes (textarea) --- ## Budget Rules ### Budget Hierarchy **Structure:** ``` Budget └── Budget Groups └── Budget Items ``` **Relations:** - One budget has many groups - One group has many items - Items belong to exactly one group - Groups belong to exactly one budget --- ### Budget Calculations **Location:** `src/pages/budget/group/BudgetGroup.tsx:33-55` **Rule:** Group totals are calculated by aggregating all items in the group. #### Income vs. Expense Handling **Key Rule:** INCOME items **subtract** from totals, EXPENSE items **add** to totals. **Rationale:** From a budget planning perspective: - Expenses increase your budget need (positive) - Income decreases your budget need (negative/offset) --- #### Total Planned Amount ```typescript const totalPlaned = budgetItems.reduce((res, currentValue) => { if (currentValue.type === CategoryTypeE.INCOME) { return res - currentValue.plannedAmount; // Subtract income } return res + currentValue.plannedAmount; // Add expense }, 0); ``` **Example:** - Item 1 (EXPENSE): $500 planned - Item 2 (INCOME): $200 planned - Item 3 (EXPENSE): $300 planned - **Total:** $500 - $200 + $300 = **$600** --- #### Total Actual Amount ```typescript const totalActual = budgetItems.reduce((res, currentValue) => { if (currentValue.type === CategoryTypeE.INCOME) { return res - currentValue.actualAmount; // Subtract income } return res + currentValue.actualAmount; // Add expense }, 0); ``` **Tracks actual spending/income against budget.** --- #### Total Remaining Amount ```typescript const totalRemaining = budgetItems.reduce((res, currentValue) => { if (currentValue.type === CategoryTypeE.INCOME) { return res - currentValue.remainingAmount; // Subtract income } return res + currentValue.remainingAmount; // Add expense }, 0); ``` **Calculated as:** `remaining = planned - actual` --- ### Budget Item Amounts **Three amount types tracked per item:** 1. **Planned Amount:** Budgeted/target amount 2. **Actual Amount:** Real transactions total 3. **Remaining Amount:** Difference (planned - actual) **Calculation (server-side):** ``` remaining = planned - actual ``` **Display:** - Positive remaining: Under budget ✅ - Negative remaining: Over budget ❌ - Zero remaining: Exactly on budget --- ### Budget Item Display Rules **Location:** `src/pages/budget/item/BudgetItem.tsx:62, 84` **Rule:** Display logic differs for INCOME vs. EXPENSE items. ```typescript {budgetItem.type === CategoryTypeE.INCOME ? ( ) : ( )} ``` **Income Items:** - Green/positive styling - Inverted display (income reduces budget need) **Expense Items:** - Red/negative styling - Normal display (expenses increase budget need) --- ## Category Rules ### Category Types **Location:** `src/shared/types/CategoryTypeE.ts` **Types:** ```typescript export enum CategoryTypeE { INCOME = 'income', EXPENSE = 'expense', } ``` **Rules:** - Every category must have a type - Type determines amount sign convention - Type affects budget calculations - Type affects transaction amount normalization --- ### User-Defined Categories **Location:** `src/pages/userCatigory/` **Rules:** - Users can create custom categories - Each category has a name, type, and optional color - Categories are reusable across transactions and budgets - Default category type: `INCOME` **Default Form Values:** ```typescript { type: [CategoryTypeE.INCOME], // Default to income } ``` --- ### Category Assignment **Transactions:** - Multiple categories can be assigned to one transaction - Field name: `categories` (array of UUIDs) - Required for transaction creation **Budget Items:** - Each budget item links to one or more categories - Tracks spending per category --- ## Validation Error Handling ### Field-Level Errors **Pattern:** ```typescript import { useController, useFormContext } from 'react-hook-form'; const { field, fieldState: { error } } = useController({ name, control, rules: { required: 'This field is required', maxLength: { value: 100, message: 'Too long' }, }, }); // Display error {error &&

{error.message}

} ``` **Error Display:** - Errors shown below input field - Red text and border styling - Cleared on user input - Prevents form submission until resolved --- ### API Error Handling **Pattern:** ```typescript try { await apiOperation(); notificationService.addAlert('Success!'); } catch (error) { notificationService.addErrorAlert(error.message); } ``` **User Experience:** - API errors displayed as toast notifications - User-friendly error messages - Console logging for debugging --- ## Data Integrity Rules ### UUID Requirements **Type:** `UuidT` (branded string type) **Usage:** - All entity IDs are UUIDs - Transaction IDs - Budget IDs - Category IDs - Bank Account IDs - User IDs **Validation:** Server-side UUID format validation --- ### Required Relationships **Transactions:** - Must link to exactly **1 bank account** - Must link to **1+ categories** - Must have **1 category type** **Budget Items:** - Must link to exactly **1 budget group** - Must link to **1+ categories** - Must have **1 category type** **Budget Groups:** - Must link to exactly **1 budget** --- ## Currency Rules **Location:** `src/shared/types/CurrencyE.ts` **Supported Currencies:** - Enum of ISO currency codes (e.g., `USD`, `EUR`, `GBP`) - Default currency per transaction - Single-select dropdown in forms **Display:** - Currency symbol or code shown with amounts - Localized formatting (future enhancement) --- ## Date Rules ### Transaction Dates **Constraints:** - Cannot be in the future - Must be valid date - Stored as ISO string **Default:** Today's date --- ### Budget Periods **Rules:** - Budgets have start and end dates - Items track actuals within budget period - Transactions matched to budgets by date range --- ## Business Logic Summary ### Key Principles 1. **Amount Sign Convention:** - EXPENSE: Negative amounts - INCOME: Positive amounts 2. **Budget Math:** - INCOME subtracts from budget - EXPENSE adds to budget - Remaining = Planned - Actual 3. **Validation:** - Client-side: Real-time pattern blocking + error display - Server-side: Full validation with error responses 4. **Data Normalization:** - Commas → periods for decimals - Amount signs normalized by category type - Dates stored as ISO strings 5. **Required Fields:** - Most entities require name and type - Relationships are enforced (bank account, categories) - Amounts require currency 6. **User Experience:** - Clear error messages - Real-time validation feedback - Toast notifications for success/error - Prevent invalid submissions --- ## Testing Business Rules ### Test Scenarios **Amount Normalization:** ```typescript it('should negate positive amount for EXPENSE', () => { const result = normalizeAmount(50, CategoryTypeE.EXPENSE); expect(result).toBe(-50); }); it('should make negative amount positive for INCOME', () => { const result = normalizeAmount(-100, CategoryTypeE.INCOME); expect(result).toBe(100); }); ``` **Budget Calculations:** ```typescript it('should subtract income from total', () => { const items = [ { type: CategoryTypeE.EXPENSE, plannedAmount: 500 }, { type: CategoryTypeE.INCOME, plannedAmount: 200 }, ]; const total = calculateTotal(items); expect(total).toBe(300); // 500 - 200 }); ``` **Validation:** ```typescript it('should reject invalid price format', () => { const result = validationPatterns.PRICE.value.test('abc'); expect(result).toBe(false); }); it('should accept valid price with comma', () => { const result = validationPatterns.PRICE.value.test('123,45'); expect(result).toBe(true); }); ``` --- ## Future Considerations ### Potential Enhancements 1. **Multi-Currency Support:** - Convert amounts between currencies - Track exchange rates - Display in user's preferred currency 2. **Recurring Transactions:** - Auto-create transactions on schedule - Templates for common transactions 3. **Budget Templates:** - Reusable budget structures - Copy from previous periods 4. **Category Budgets:** - Set limits per category - Alerts when exceeding 5. **Advanced Validation:** - Email format validation - Password strength requirements - Custom validation rules per user 6. **Audit Trail:** - Track changes to transactions/budgets - Display modification history - Rollback capabilities

// Modal content 303→ name: ModalNameE; // Modal identifier 304→ title: string; // Modal title 305→ type?: ModalTypeE; // Modal variant (REGULAR) 306→}; 307→``` 308→ 309→**Key Features:** 310→ 311→- Uses native `` element with `showModal()` 312→- Subscribes to global modals state via `useGlobalValue` 313→- Automatically closes on unmount 314→- Closes on outside click (via `useOutsideClickListener`) 315→- Manages open/close based on presence in global state 316→- Uses `modalService` for state management 317→ 318→**Usage Example:** 319→ 320→```typescript 321→import Modal from 'Components/modal/Modal'; 331→modalService.open(ModalNameE.TRANSACTION); 332→``` 333→ 334→**Dependencies:** `modalService`, `useGlobalValue`, `useOutsideClickListener` 335→ 336→**Related Files:** 337→ 338→- `ModalNameE.ts` - Enum of modal names 339→- `ModalPropsT.ts` - Type-safe props for each modal 340→- `ModalTypeE.ts` - Modal variants 341→ 342→--- 343→ 344→### ErrorModal 345→ 346→**Location:** `src/components/modal/errorModal/ErrorModal.tsx` 347→ 348→**Purpose:** Displays API error messages in a modal dialog with user-friendly messaging. 349→ 350→**Props:** None (uses `useModalData` hook to get message from modal state) 351→ 352→**Key Features:** 353→ 354→- Uses `useModalData(ModalNameE.ERROR)` to get error message 355→- Displays message in a paragraph with `role="alert"` and `aria-live="assertive"` 356→- Close button dismisses modal via `modalService.close(ModalNameE.ERROR)` 357→- Uses `ModalTypeE.ERROR` for error-specific styling 358→ 359→**Usage Example:** 360→ 361→```typescript 362→// ErrorModal is rendered globally in App.tsx 363→// To display an error modal, use modalService: 364→import { modalService } from 'Services/modalService'; 365→import { ModalNameE } from 'Components/modal/ModalNameE'; 366→ 367→modalService.open(ModalNameE.ERROR, { message: 'Something went wrong.' }); 368→ 369→// To close: 370→modalService.close(ModalNameE.ERROR); // Close callback 402→ title?: string; // Sidebar title 403→ size?: SidebarSizeE; // REGULAR or other sizes 404→}; 405→``` 406→ 407→**Key Features:** 408→ 409→- Renders via React Portal to `#sidebar_portal` 410→- Subscribes to global sidebar state 411→- Supports stacking (multiple sidebars open) 412→- Graceful closing animations via `deleted` flag 413→- Automatically closes on unmount 414→- Closes on backdrop click 415→- Scroll lock for REGULAR size (prevents body scroll) 416→- Back button support 417→- Header actions area 418→- Calls `sidebarService.finalizeClose()` after animation ends 419→ 420→**Usage Example:** 421→ 422→```typescript 423→import Sidebar from 'Components/sidebar/Sidebar'; `GrabjectSvg` - Grab handle for drag-and-drop 649→- `HamburgerSvg` - Hamburger menu icon 650→- `LogoutSvg` - Logout icon 651→- `PencilSvg` - Pencil edit icon 652→- `PlusSvg` - Plus icon 653→- 666→ 667→ 668→``` 669→ 670→**Key Features:** 671→ 672→- Consistent sizing 673→- Themeable via CSS 674→- Accessible with proper aria labels 675→- Can be used in buttons and inline 676→ 677→--- 678→ 679→## Component Naming Conventions 680→ 681→### Form Components 682→ 683→- **Always** prefix with `Au` 684→- PascalCase: `AuButton`, `AuField`, `AuSelect` 685→- Located in `src/components/form/` 686→ 687→### Page Components 688→ 689→- Suffix with `Page` 690→- Example: `TransactionsPage`, `BudgetPage` 691→- Located in `src/pages/` 692→ 693→### Sidebar Components 694→ 695→- Suffix with `Sidebar` 696→- Example: `EditBudgetSidebar`, `TransactionFormSidebar` 697→- Located in page directories: `src/pages/budget/sidebars/` 698→ 699→### Layout Components 700→ 701→- Suffix with `Layout` 702→- Example: `MainLayout`, `BaseLayout` 703→- Located in `src/components/layouts/` 704→ 705→### SVG Components 706→ 707→- Suffix with `Svg` or `SVG` 708→- Example: `PencilSvg`, `CloseSvg` 709→- Located in `src/components/svg/` 710→ 711→--- 712→ 713→## Component Styling & Theme Support 714→ 715→### How Component Styling Works 716→ 717→All components use SCSS modules with semantic CSS variables for automatic theme support. 718→ 719→**Key Principles:** 720→ 721→1. **Semantic Variables Only** - Use `var(--color-text)`, not hardcoded colors 722→2. **No `!important`** - CSS variables inherit naturally 723→3. **No Manual Theme Logic** - No `@media (prefers-color-scheme)` in component files 724→4. **Light Theme First** - Default values at `:root`, overrides in media query 725→ 726→### Semantic Variables Available 727→ 728→All components have access to these variables: 729→ 730→```scss 731→// Text colors 732→--color-text // Primary text (black in light, light gray in dark) 733→--color-text-subtle // Secondary text (gray-2 in light, gray-5 in dark) 734→--color-text-muted // Tertiary text (gray-1 in light, gray-6 in dark) 735→ 736→// Backgrounds 737→--color-background // Page background (primary-blue-light in light, #0f1419 in dark) 738→--color-foreground // Component background (white in light, #1a1f26 in dark) 739→ 740→// Borders 741→--color-border // Primary border (gray-4 in light, gray-7 in dark) 742→--color-border-subtle // Secondary border (gray-3 in light, gray-8 in dark) 743→ 744→// Interactive elements 745→--color-button-primary // Button background 746→--color-button-primary-hover // Button hover 747→--color-button-primary-active // Button active/pressed 748→--color-button-text-on-primary // Text on dark button background 749→--color-input-background // Input field background 750→--color-input-border // Input field border 751→--color-input-border-hover // Input on hover 752→--color-input-border-active // Input on focus 753→--color-placeholder // Placeholder text 754→ 755→// States 756→--color-disabled-bg // Disabled element background 757→--color-disabled-text // Disabled element text 758→--color-checkbox-unchecked // Checkbox unchecked state 759→--color-avatar-placeholder // Avatar placeholder color 760→ 761→// Links 762→--color-link // Link text 763→--color-link-hover // Link on hover 764→``` 765→ 766→See [PATTERNS.md#9-theme-system-pattern](./PATTERNS.md#9-theme-system-pattern) for complete list. 767→ 768→### Example: Styling a Component 769→ 770→```scss 771→// src/components/form/button/auButton.scss 772→.button { 773→ // Always use semantic variables 774→ background-color: var(--color-button-primary); 775→ color: var(--color-button-text-on-primary); 776→ border: none; 777→ 778→ &:hover { 779→ background-color: var(--color-button-primary-hover); 780→ } 781→ 782→ &:active { 783→ background-color: var(--color-button-primary-active); 784→ } 785→ 786→ &:disabled { 787→ background-color: var(--color-disabled-bg); 788→ color: var(--color-disabled-text); 789→ cursor: not-allowed; 790→ } 791→} 792→``` 793→ 794→### Theme Behavior 795→ 796→**Automatic theme switching:** 797→ 798→1. Component uses semantic variables: `background-color: var(--color-button-primary)` 799→2. Light mode (default): `--color-button-primary: #075eff` (bright blue) 800→3. Dark mode (OS preference): `--color-button-primary: #5a95ff` (softer blue) 801→4. No component code changes needed - CSS variables handle it! 802→ 803→**Browser updates colors automatically when:** 804→ 805→- User changes OS theme preference 806→- User changes browser theme setting 807→- No page refresh required - instant update 808→ 809→### What NOT to Do 810→ 811→```scss 812→// ❌ NEVER use hardcoded colors 813→.button { 814→ background-color: #075eff; // WRONG - won't adapt to dark theme 815→} 816→ 817→// ❌ NEVER use specific color variables 818→.button { 819→ background-color: var(--color-primary-blue); // WRONG - use semantic instead 820→} 821→ 822→// ❌ NEVER add @media (prefers-color-scheme) in components 823→// This is handled globally in src/app/styles/variables.scss 824→.button { 825→ @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) { 826→ background-color: #5a95ff; // WRONG - don't do this 827→ } 828→} 829→ 830→// ❌ NEVER mix hardcoded colors with variables 831→.button { 832→ background-color: var(--color-button-primary); 833→ border: 1px solid #cccccc; // WRONG - should use var(--color-border) 834→} 835→``` 836→ 837→--- 838→ 839→## Component Patterns 840→ 841→### 1. Form Integration Pattern 842→ 843→Components that integrate with react-hook-form: 844→ 845→```typescript 846→import { useFormContext, useController } from 'react-hook-form'; 847→ 848→const MyFormComponent = ({ name }: { name: string }) => { 849→ const { control } = useFormContext(); 850→ const { field, fieldState: { error } } = useController({ 851→ name, 852→ control, 853→ rules: { required: 'Required' } 854→ }); 855→ 856→ return ( 857→
858→ 859→ {error && {error.message}} 860→
861→ ); 862→}; 863→``` 864→ 865→### 2. Global State Subscription Pattern 866→ 867→Components that read from global state: 868→ 869→```typescript 870→import { useGlobalValue } from 'Shared/store/useGlobalValue'; 871→import { GlobalStoreFieldE } from 'Shared/types/GlobalStoreFieldE'; 872→ 873→const MyComponent = () => { 874→ const sidebars = useGlobalValue(GlobalStoreFieldE.SIDEBAR); 875→ 876→ return
{sidebars.length} open
; 877→}; 878→``` 879→ 880→### 3. Service-Managed UI Pattern 881→ 882→Components managed by services (modals, sidebars): 883→ 884→```typescript 885→// Component definition 886→const MySidebar = () => { 887→ const sidebars = useGlobalValue(GlobalStoreFieldE.SIDEBAR); 888→ const props = sidebars.find(s => s.name === SidebarNameE.MY_SIDEBAR)?.props; 889→ 890→ if (!props) return null; 891→ 892→ return ( 893→ 894→ Content 895→ 896→ ); 897→}; 898→ 899→// Usage - open from anywhere 900→sidebarService.openFirst(SidebarNameE.MY_SIDEBAR, { id: 'uuid' }); 901→``` 902→ 903→### 4. Portal Pattern 904→ 905→Components that render outside their parent DOM hierarchy: 906→ 907→```typescript 908→import { createPortal } from 'react-dom'; 909→ 910→const container = document.getElementById('portal_root'); 911→ 912→const MyPortalComponent = () => { 913→ return createPortal( 914→
Portal Content
, 915→ container!, 916→ 'unique-key' 917→ ); 918→}; 919→``` 920→ 921→--- 922→ 923→## Testing Components 924→ 925→Components should have tests in `__tests__` directories adjacent to the component: 926→ 927→``` 928→src/components/form/button/ 929→ ├── AuButton.tsx 930→ ├── AuButtonKindE.ts 931→ ├── AuButtonSizeE.ts 932→ ├── auButton.scss 933→ └── __tests__/ 934→ └── AuButton.test.tsx 935→``` 936→ 937→**Test Guidelines:** 938→ 939→- Test user interactions 940→- Test conditional rendering 941→- Test accessibility 942→- Don't test simple prop passing 943→- Use `@testing-library/react` for component tests 944→ 945→--- 946→ 947→## Accessibility Guidelines 948→ 949→All components should follow these accessibility practices: 950→ 951→1. **Semantic HTML**: Use appropriate HTML elements (` 968→``` 969→ Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. # Component Documentation Comprehensive guide to all React components in the application. ## Form Components (`src/components/form/`) ### AuButton **Location:** `src/components/form/button/AuButton.tsx` **Purpose:** Flexible button component with multiple variants and sizes. **Props:** ```typescript type PropsT = { areaPressed?: boolean; className?: string; children: React.ReactNode; form?: string; // Form ID for submit buttons kind?: AuButtonKindE; // Button variant onClick?: () => void; size?: AuButtonSizeE; // Button size testId?: string; type?: 'submit' | 'reset' | 'button'; disabled?: boolean; }; ``` **Key Features:** - Six button variants via `AuButtonKindE`: PRIMARY, SECONDARY, LINK, ICON, CHIPS, TRANSPARENT - Three sizes via `AuButtonSizeE`: SMALL, MEDIUM, LARGE - Prevents default on click (calls `e.preventDefault()`) - Accessible with aria-pressed support - Default type is 'button' (prevents accidental form submission) **Usage Example:** ```typescript import AuButton from 'Components/form/button/AuButton'; import { AuButtonKindE } from 'Components/form/button/AuButtonKindE'; import { AuButtonSizeE } from 'Components/form/button/AuButtonSizeE'; handleSubmit()} > Save Changes ``` **Dependencies:** `classnames` --- ### AuField **Location:** `src/components/form/field/AuField.tsx` **Purpose:** Form field wrapper that integrates with react-hook-form, providing label, input, validation, and error display. **Props:** ```typescript type MyFieldProps = { children: React.ReactElement; // Input element to wrap className?: string; clearValue?: AuFieldClearValueT; // Value for clear button defaultFocus?: boolean; // Auto-focus on mount htmlFor?: string; maxLength?: number; minLength?: number; name: string; // Required - field name label?: string; type?: string; placeholder?: string; pattern?: ValidationPatternT; // Regex validation replace?: ReplacePatternT; // Replace pattern on input required?: boolean; withoutValidation?: boolean; // Skip validation }; ``` **Key Features:** - Integrates with react-hook-form via `useController` - Built-in validation: required, minLength, maxLength, pattern - Optional clear button when value differs from `clearValue` - Real-time pattern replacement on input - Blocks invalid input based on pattern - Auto-focus support - Error display below input - Warns in dev mode if child props are overridden **Usage Example:** ```typescript import AuField from 'Components/form/field/AuField'; import { useFormContext } from 'react-hook-form'; // Inside a FormProvider ``` **Dependencies:** `react-hook-form`, `classnames`, `AuButton`, `ClearSvg` --- ### AuDropdown **Location:** `src/components/form/dropdown/AuDropdown.tsx` **Purpose:** Dropdown menu component for displaying action menus. **Usage:** Typically used for action menus in table rows or cards. **Dependencies:** Position-based dropdown with click-outside detection --- ### AuSelect **Location:** `src/components/form/select/AuSelect.tsx` **Purpose:** Select dropdown for choosing from options. **Props:** Accepts options as `SelectOptionT[]` type. **Usage:** Form select with react-hook-form integration. --- ### AuCheckbox **Location:** `src/components/form/checkbox/AuCheckbox.tsx` **Purpose:** Checkbox input component. **Usage:** Integrated with react-hook-form for boolean values. --- ### AuRadioGroup **Location:** `src/components/form/radioGroup/AuRadioGroup.tsx` **Purpose:** Radio button group component. **Usage:** Multiple choice selection with react-hook-form. --- ### AuDatePicker **Location:** `src/components/form/datePicker/AuDatePicker.tsx` **Purpose:** Date picker component. **Usage:** Date input with calendar UI. --- ### AuForm **Location:** `src/components/form/AuForm.tsx` **Purpose:** Form container that provides react-hook-form context. **Usage:** Wraps form fields with FormProvider. --- ### AuFormBlock **Location:** `src/components/form/formBlock/AuFormBlock.tsx` **Purpose:** Form section wrapper for grouping related fields. **Usage:** Visual grouping of form fields. --- ## Layout Components (`src/components/layouts/`) ### MainLayout **Location:** `src/components/layouts/main/MainLayout.tsx` **Purpose:** Main application layout with navigation menu, content area, footer, and AI assistant. **Props:** ```typescript type PropsT = { className?: string; children?: ReactNode; // Page content }; ``` **Key Features:** - Wraps content in `BaseLayout` - Includes `Menu` component in nav - Includes footer with logo - Includes `AIAssistant` component - Applies custom className to main content area **Structure:** ``` BaseLayout ├──
Menu ├──
{children} ├──
Logo └── AIAssistant ``` **Usage Example:** ```typescript import MainLayout from 'Components/layouts/main/MainLayout'; const MyPage = () => (

Page Content

 ); ``` **Dependencies:** `BaseLayout`, `Menu`, `AIAssistant` --- ### BaseLayout **Location:** `src/components/layouts/base/BaseLayout.tsx` **Purpose:** Base layout wrapper that provides fundamental structure. **Usage:** Usually not used directly - consumed by `MainLayout`. --- ### Menu **Location:** `src/components/layouts/main/menu/Menu.tsx` **Purpose:** Navigation menu component. **Key Features:** - Contains `Navigation` links - Includes `LogoutButton` --- ### Navigation **Location:** `src/components/layouts/main/menu/navigation/Navigation.tsx` **Purpose:** Navigation links for main pages. **Features:** Links to Transactions, Budgets, Bank Accounts, Categories, Profile --- ### LogoutButton **Location:** `src/components/layouts/main/menu/logoutButton/LogoutButton.tsx` **Purpose:** Logout button in menu. **Features:** Calls auth service to log out user --- ## Modal System (`src/components/modal/`) ### Modal **Location:** `src/components/modal/Modal.tsx` **Purpose:** Modal dialog component that uses HTML `` element and global state management. **Props:** ```typescript type PropsT = { children: ReactNode; // Modal content name: ModalNameE; // Modal identifier title: string; // Modal title type?: ModalTypeE; // Modal variant (REGULAR) }; ``` **Key Features:** - Uses native `` element with `showModal()` - Subscribes to global modals state via `useGlobalValue` - Automatically closes on unmount - Closes on outside click (via `useOutsideClickListener`) - Manages open/close based on presence in global state - Uses `modalService` for state management **Usage Example:** ```typescript import Modal from 'Components/modal/Modal'; import { ModalNameE } from 'Components/modal/ModalNameE'; // Modal component (rendered in app root) // Open modal from anywhere import { modalService } from 'Services/modalService'; modalService.open(ModalNameE.TRANSACTION); ``` **Dependencies:** `modalService`, `useGlobalValue`, `useOutsideClickListener` **Related Files:** - `ModalNameE.ts` - Enum of modal names - `ModalPropsT.ts` - Type-safe props for each modal - `ModalTypeE.ts` - Modal variants --- ### ErrorModal **Location:** `src/components/modal/errorModal/ErrorModal.tsx` **Purpose:** Displays API error messages in a modal dialog with user-friendly messaging. **Props:** None (uses `useModalData` hook to get message from modal state) **Key Features:** - Uses `useModalData(ModalNameE.ERROR)` to get error message - Displays message in a paragraph with `role="alert"` and `aria-live="assertive"` - Close button dismisses modal via `modalService.close(ModalNameE.ERROR)` - Uses `ModalTypeE.ERROR` for error-specific styling **Usage Example:** ```typescript // ErrorModal is rendered globally in App.tsx // To display an error modal, use modalService: import { modalService } from 'Services/modalService'; import { ModalNameE } from 'Components/modal/ModalNameE'; modalService.open(ModalNameE.ERROR, { message: 'Something went wrong.' }); // To close: modalService.close(ModalNameE.ERROR); ``` **Related Files:** - `errorModal.css` - Error modal styles - `src/shared/requestClient/errorInterceptor.ts` - Triggers modal on API errors **Tests:** - Unit: `ErrorModal.test.tsx` --- ## Sidebar System (`src/components/sidebar/`) ### Sidebar **Location:** `src/components/sidebar/Sidebar.tsx` **Purpose:** Sliding sidebar panel with animations, stacking support, and scroll lock management. **Props:** ```typescript type PropsT = { children: ReactNode; // Sidebar content className?: string; headerActions?: ReactNode; // Additional header buttons name: SidebarNameE; // Sidebar identifier onBack?: () => void; // Back button handler onClose?: () => void; // Close callback title?: string; // Sidebar title size?: SidebarSizeE; // REGULAR or other sizes }; ``` **Key Features:** - Renders via React Portal to `#sidebar_portal` - Subscribes to global sidebar state - Supports stacking (multiple sidebars open) - Graceful closing animations via `deleted` flag - Automatically closes on unmount - Closes on backdrop click - Scroll lock for REGULAR size (prevents body scroll) - Back button support - Header actions area - Calls `sidebarService.finalizeClose()` after animation ends **Usage Example:** ```typescript import Sidebar from 'Components/sidebar/Sidebar'; import { SidebarNameE } from 'Components/sidebar/types/SidebarNameE'; import { SidebarSizeE } from 'Components/sidebar/types/SidebarSizeE'; // Sidebar component const EditBudgetSidebar = () => { const sidebars = useGlobalValue(GlobalStoreFieldE.SIDEBAR); const props = sidebars.find(s => s.name === SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET)?.props; if (!props) return null; return ( console.log('Closed')} > ); }; // Open from anywhere import { sidebarService } from 'Services/sidebarService'; sidebarService.openFirst(SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET, { budgetId: 'uuid' }); ``` **Dependencies:** `sidebarService`, `scrollLockService`, `useGlobalValue`, `createPortal` **Related Files:** - `SidebarNameE.ts` - Enum of sidebar names - `SidebarPropsT.ts` - Type-safe props for each sidebar - `SidebarSizeE.ts` - Sidebar size variants - `SidebarLayout.tsx` - Layout wrapper for sidebar content --- ### SidebarLayout **Location:** `src/components/sidebar/SidebarLayout.tsx` **Purpose:** Layout wrapper for sidebar content with consistent structure. **Usage:** Wraps sidebar forms and content for consistent padding and structure. --- ## UI Components ### PageHeader **Location:** `src/components/pageHeader/PageHeader.tsx` **Purpose:** Page header component with title and optional actions. **Props:** ```typescript type PropsT = { children?: React.ReactNode; // Action buttons title: string; // Page title }; ``` **Key Features:** - Displays page title as `

` - Optional actions area on the right - Consistent styling across pages **Usage Example:** ```typescript import PageHeader from 'Components/pageHeader/PageHeader'; import AuButton from 'Components/form/button/AuButton'; New Transaction ``` **Dependencies:** None (pure presentation component) --- ### Spinner **Location:** `src/components/spinner/Spinner.tsx` **Purpose:** Loading spinner component. **Usage:** Display loading state during async operations. --- ### EmptyState **Location:** `src/components/emptyState/EmptyState.tsx` **Purpose:** Empty state placeholder component. **Usage:** Display when lists or data are empty. --- ### CategoryBadge **Location:** `src/components/categoryBage/CategoryBadge.tsx` **Purpose:** Badge component for displaying category with color. **Usage:** Show category in transaction lists and details. --- ### EditableLabel **Location:** `src/components/editableLabel/EditableLabel.tsx` **Purpose:** Inline editable label component. **Usage:** Edit text in place (e.g., budget group names). --- ### ActionDropdown **Location:** `src/components/actionDropdown/ActionDropdown.tsx` **Purpose:** Dropdown menu for row actions. **Usage:** Edit/Delete actions in table rows. --- ## AI Assistant Components (`src/components/aiAssistant/`) ### AIAssistant **Location:** `src/components/aiAssistant/AIAssistant.tsx` **Purpose:** Main AI assistant container with chat bubble and window. **Key Features:** - Toggles between bubble and chat window - Uses `useToggle` hook for state - Positioned fixed on page (via CSS) **Structure:** ```typescript
{isOpen && }
``` **Usage:** Automatically included in `MainLayout`. **Dependencies:** `ChatBubble`, `ChatWindow`, `useToggle` --- ### ChatBubble **Location:** `src/components/aiAssistant/chatBubble/ChatBubble.tsx` **Purpose:** Floating chat bubble button to open AI assistant. **Props:** - `onClick`: Opens chat window - `isOpen`: Current state **Usage:** Rendered in `AIAssistant` component. --- ### ChatWindow **Location:** `src/components/aiAssistant/chatWindow/ChatWindow.tsx` **Purpose:** Chat window for AI assistant conversations. **Props:** - `onClose`: Closes chat window **Key Features:** - Displays conversation history - Input for new messages - Integrates with `aiAssistantService` --- ### MessageContent **Location:** `src/components/aiAssistant/chatWindow/MessageContent.tsx` **Purpose:** Renders individual chat messages. **Usage:** Renders user and assistant messages with formatting. --- ## SVG Icon Components (`src/components/svg/`) **Location:** `src/components/svg/` **Purpose:** SVG icon components for consistent iconography. **Icons:** - `BackSvg` - Back arrow - `CalendarSvg` - Calendar icon - `ClearSvg` - Clear/X icon - `CloseSvg` - Close icon - `CopySvg` - Copy icon - `EditSvg` - Edit/Pen icon - `FileImportSvg` - File import icon - `FilterSvg` - Filter icon - `GrabjectSvg` - Grab handle for drag-and-drop - `HamburgerSvg` - Hamburger menu icon - `LogoutSvg` - Logout icon - `PencilSvg` - Pencil edit icon - `PlusSvg` - Plus icon - `ReloadSVG` - Reload icon - `TrashSvg` - Delete/Trash icon - `TriangleSVG` - Triangle icon - `Svg` - Base SVG wrapper component **Usage Example:** ```typescript import PencilSvg from 'Components/svg/PencilSvg'; import AuButton from 'Components/form/button/AuButton'; import { AuButtonKindE } from 'Components/form/button/AuButtonKindE'; ``` **Key Features:** - Consistent sizing - Themeable via CSS - Accessible with proper aria labels - Can be used in buttons and inline --- ## Component Naming Conventions ### Form Components - **Always** prefix with `Au` - PascalCase: `AuButton`, `AuField`, `AuSelect` - Located in `src/components/form/` ### Page Components - Suffix with `Page` - Example: `TransactionsPage`, `BudgetPage` - Located in `src/pages/` ### Sidebar Components - Suffix with `Sidebar` - Example: `EditBudgetSidebar`, `TransactionFormSidebar` - Located in page directories: `src/pages/budget/sidebars/` ### Layout Components - Suffix with `Layout` - Example: `MainLayout`, `BaseLayout` - Located in `src/components/layouts/` ### SVG Components - Suffix with `Svg` or `SVG` - Example: `PencilSvg`, `CloseSvg` - Located in `src/components/svg/` --- ## Component Styling & Theme Support ### How Component Styling Works All components use SCSS modules with semantic CSS variables for automatic theme support. **Key Principles:** 1. **Semantic Variables Only** - Use `var(--color-text)`, not hardcoded colors 2. **No `!important`** - CSS variables inherit naturally 3. **No Manual Theme Logic** - No `@media (prefers-color-scheme)` in component files 4. **Light Theme First** - Default values at `:root`, overrides in media query ### Semantic Variables Available All components have access to these variables: ```scss // Text colors --color-text // Primary text (black in light, light gray in dark) --color-text-subtle // Secondary text (gray-2 in light, gray-5 in dark) --color-text-muted // Tertiary text (gray-1 in light, gray-6 in dark) // Backgrounds --color-background // Page background (primary-blue-light in light, #0f1419 in dark) --color-foreground // Component background (white in light, #1a1f26 in dark) // Borders --color-border // Primary border (gray-4 in light, gray-7 in dark) --color-border-subtle // Secondary border (gray-3 in light, gray-8 in dark) // Interactive elements --color-button-primary // Button background --color-button-primary-hover // Button hover --color-button-primary-active // Button active/pressed --color-button-text-on-primary // Text on dark button background --color-input-background // Input field background --color-input-border // Input field border --color-input-border-hover // Input on hover --color-input-border-active // Input on focus --color-placeholder // Placeholder text // States --color-disabled-bg // Disabled element background --color-disabled-text // Disabled element text --color-checkbox-unchecked // Checkbox unchecked state --color-avatar-placeholder // Avatar placeholder color // Links --color-link // Link text --color-link-hover // Link on hover ``` See [PATTERNS.md#9-theme-system-pattern](./PATTERNS.md#9-theme-system-pattern) for complete list. ### Example: Styling a Component ```scss // src/components/form/button/auButton.scss .button { // Always use semantic variables background-color: var(--color-button-primary); color: var(--color-button-text-on-primary); border: none; &:hover { background-color: var(--color-button-primary-hover); } &:active { background-color: var(--color-button-primary-active); } &:disabled { background-color: var(--color-disabled-bg); color: var(--color-disabled-text); cursor: not-allowed; } } ``` ### Theme Behavior **Automatic theme switching:** 1. Component uses semantic variables: `background-color: var(--color-button-primary)` 2. Light mode (default): `--color-button-primary: #075eff` (bright blue) 3. Dark mode (OS preference): `--color-button-primary: #5a95ff` (softer blue) 4. No component code changes needed - CSS variables handle it! **Browser updates colors automatically when:** - User changes OS theme preference - User changes browser theme setting - No page refresh required - instant update ### What NOT to Do ```scss // ❌ NEVER use hardcoded colors .button { background-color: #075eff; // WRONG - won't adapt to dark theme } // ❌ NEVER use specific color variables .button { background-color: var(--color-primary-blue); // WRONG - use semantic instead } // ❌ NEVER add @media (prefers-color-scheme) in components // This is handled globally in src/app/styles/variables.scss .button { @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) { background-color: #5a95ff; // WRONG - don't do this } } // ❌ NEVER mix hardcoded colors with variables .button { background-color: var(--color-button-primary); border: 1px solid #cccccc; // WRONG - should use var(--color-border) } ``` --- ## Component Patterns ### 1. Form Integration Pattern Components that integrate with react-hook-form: ```typescript import { useFormContext, useController } from 'react-hook-form'; const MyFormComponent = ({ name }: { name: string }) => { const { control } = useFormContext(); const { field, fieldState: { error } } = useController({ name, control, rules: { required: 'Required' } }); return (
{error && {error.message}}
); }; ``` ### 2. Global State Subscription Pattern Components that read from global state: ```typescript import { useGlobalValue } from 'Shared/store/useGlobalValue'; import { GlobalStoreFieldE } from 'Shared/types/GlobalStoreFieldE'; const MyComponent = () => { const sidebars = useGlobalValue(GlobalStoreFieldE.SIDEBAR); return
{sidebars.length} open
; }; ``` ### 3. Service-Managed UI Pattern Components managed by services (modals, sidebars): ```typescript // Component definition const MySidebar = () => { const sidebars = useGlobalValue(GlobalStoreFieldE.SIDEBAR); const props = sidebars.find(s => s.name === SidebarNameE.MY_SIDEBAR)?.props; if (!props) return null; return ( Content ); }; // Usage - open from anywhere sidebarService.openFirst(SidebarNameE.MY_SIDEBAR, { id: 'uuid' }); ``` ### 4. Portal Pattern Components that render outside their parent DOM hierarchy: ```typescript import { createPortal } from 'react-dom'; const container = document.getElementById('portal_root'); const MyPortalComponent = () => { return createPortal(
Portal Content
, container!, 'unique-key' ); }; ``` --- ## Testing Components Components should have tests in `__tests__` directories adjacent to the component: ``` src/components/form/button/ ├── AuButton.tsx ├── AuButtonKindE.ts ├── AuButtonSizeE.ts ├── auButton.scss └── __tests__/ └── AuButton.test.tsx ``` **Test Guidelines:** - Test user interactions - Test conditional rendering - Test accessibility - Don't test simple prop passing - Use `@testing-library/react` for component tests --- ## Accessibility Guidelines All components should follow these accessibility practices: 1. **Semantic HTML**: Use appropriate HTML elements (` ```
