1→# Code Patterns 2→ 3→**Purpose:** Practical guide on how to implement features using established patterns. For understanding why these patterns exist, see [ARCHITECTURE.md](./ARCHITECTURE.md). 4→ 5→--- 6→ 7→## 1. Modal Pattern 8→ 9→### How It Works 10→ 11→Modals are managed through `modalService` which uses global state to control modal lifecycle. Each modal has a name (enum) and optional typed props. 12→ 13→**Service Methods:** 14→ 15→- `open(modalName, props?)` - Opens a modal with optional props 16→- `close(modalName)` - Closes a specific modal 17→ 18→**Implementation:** 19→ 20→- Modals stack on top of each other 21→- Props are type-safe based on modal name 22→- State stored in `GlobalStoreFieldE.MODALS` 23→ 24→### When to Use 25→ 26→- Confirmation dialogs 27→- Small forms or quick actions 28→- Alerts and notifications requiring user acknowledgment 29→ 30→### Usage Example 31→ 32→```typescript 33→import { modalService } from 'Services/modalService'; 34→import { ModalNameE } from 'Components/modal/ModalNameE'; 35→ 36→// Open modal with props 37→modalService.open(ModalNameE.TRANSACTION, { transactionId: 'uuid' }); 38→ 39→// Close modal 40→modalService.close(ModalNameE.TRANSACTION); 41→``` 42→ 43→### Example Files 44→ 45→- Service: `src/services/modalService.ts` 46→- Modal names: `src/components/modal/ModalNameE.ts` 47→- Modal props: `src/components/modal/ModalPropsT.ts` 48→- Modal component: `src/components/modal/Modal.tsx` 49→ 50→--- 51→ 52→## 2. Sidebar Pattern 53→ 54→### How It Works 55→ 56→Sidebars are managed through `sidebarService` with support for stacking and graceful animations. Each sidebar has a name (enum) and typed props. 57→ 58→**Service Methods:** 59→ 60→- `open(sidebarName, props?)` - Opens sidebar, adds to stack (removes duplicate if exists) 61→- `openFirst(sidebarName, props?)` - Opens sidebar, clears entire stack first 62→- `close(sidebarName)` - Marks sidebar as deleted (triggers close animation) 63→- `closeAll()` - Marks all sidebars as deleted 64→- `finalizeClose(sidebarName)` - Removes sidebar from state after animation completes 65→ 66→**Implementation:** 67→ 68→- Sidebars support stacking (multiple open at once) 69→- Graceful closing with animations via `deleted` flag 70→- Props are type-safe based on sidebar name 71→- State stored in `GlobalStoreFieldE.SIDEBAR` 72→ 73→### When to Use 74→ 75→- Forms (create, edit) 76→- Detail views 77→- Multi-step workflows 78→- Delete confirmations 79→- Any panel that slides in from the side 80→ 81→### Usage Example 82→ 83→```typescript 84→import { sidebarService } from 'Services/sidebarService'; 85→import { SidebarNameE } from 'Components/sidebar/types/SidebarNameE'; 86→ 87→// Open sidebar and clear stack 88→sidebarService.openFirst(SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET_GROUP, { 89→ budgetGroup: budgetGroup, 90→}); 91→ 92→// Open sidebar and add to stack 93→sidebarService.open(SidebarNameE.CREATE_BUDGET_ITEM); 94→ 95→// Close specific sidebar (triggers animation) 96→sidebarService.close(SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET_GROUP); 97→ 98→// Close all sidebars 99→sidebarService.closeAll(); 100→ 101→// Finalize close after animation (called by Sidebar component) 102→sidebarService.finalizeClose(SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET_GROUP); 103→``` 104→ 105→### Example Files 106→ 107→- Service: `src/services/sidebarService.ts` 108→- Sidebar names: `src/components/sidebar/types/SidebarNameE.ts` 109→- Sidebar props: `src/components/sidebar/types/SidebarPropsT.ts` 110→- Sidebar component: `src/components/sidebar/Sidebar.tsx` 111→- Usage example: `src/pages/budget/group/BudgetGroup.tsx:72` 112→ 113→--- 114→ 115→## 3. State Management Pattern 116→ 117→### How It Works 118→ 119→Global state uses `@preact/signals-react` for reactive state management. State is defined in `globalStore` and accessed/updated through utilities. 120→ 121→**Core Components:** 122→ 123→- `globalStore` - Central store with signals for each field 124→- `useGlobalValue(field)` - Hook to read state in components 125→- `createGlobalValueSetter(field)` - Creates updater function for services 126→ 127→**Implementation:** 128→ 129→- Each field is a `signal` that can be subscribed to 130→- React components subscribe using `useGlobalValue` 131→- Services update state using setter functions 132→- Changes automatically trigger re-renders in subscribed components 133→ 134→### When to Use 135→ 136→- Auth token 137→- Notifications 138→- Modals and sidebars state 139→- IDs lists (e.g., deleted items for optimistic updates) 140→- Any state shared across multiple components 141→ 142→### Usage Example 143→ 144→```typescript 145→// In a service 146→import { createGlobalValueSetter } from 'Shared/store/createGlobalValueSetter'; 147→import { GlobalStoreFieldE } from 'Shared/types/GlobalStoreFieldE'; 148→ 149→const updateNotifications = createGlobalValueSetter( 150→ GlobalStoreFieldE.NOTIFICATIONS, 151→); 152→ 153→export const notificationService = { 154→ add: notification => { 155→ updateNotifications(current => [...current, notification]); 156→ }, 157→}; 158→ 159→// In a component 160→import { useGlobalValue } from 'Shared/store/useGlobalValue'; 161→import { GlobalStoreFieldE } from 'Shared/types/GlobalStoreFieldE'; 162→ 163→const MyComponent = () => { 164→ const notifications = useGlobalValue(GlobalStoreFieldE.NOTIFICATIONS); 165→ 166→ return
{notifications.length} notifications
; 167→}; 168→``` 169→ 170→### Example Files 171→ 172→- Store: `src/shared/store/globalStore.ts` 173→- Hook: `src/shared/store/useGlobalValue.ts` 174→- Setter: `src/shared/store/createGlobalValueSetter.ts` 175→- Field names: `src/shared/types/GlobalStoreFieldE.ts` 176→- Usage in service: `src/services/sidebarService.ts:6` 177→ 178→--- 179→ 180→## 4. Data Fetching Pattern 181→ 182→### How It Works 183→ 184→Data fetching uses `@tanstack/react-query` with repository hooks that wrap API calls. Each repository hook handles a specific operation. 185→ 186→**Naming Convention:** 187→ 188→- `useLoadX` - Fetch single item or list 189→- `useCreateX` - Create new item 190→- `useEditX` - Update existing item 191→- `useDeleteX` - Delete item 192→ 193→**Implementation:** 194→ 195→- All API calls go through `requestClient` (Axios wrapper) 196→- Hooks use `useQuery` (read) or `useMutation` (write) 197→- Query keys based on `ApiGatewayE` enum 198→- Automatic cache invalidation after mutations 199→- Infinite queries for paginated lists 200→ 201→### When to Use 202→ 203→- All API interactions 204→- Server state management 205→- Caching and background updates 206→- Optimistic updates 207→ 208→### Query Hook Example (Read) 209→ 210→```typescript 211→import { useInfiniteQuery } from '@tanstack/react-query'; 212→import { requestClient } from 'Shared/requestClient/requestClient'; 213→import { ApiGatewayE } from 'Shared/types/api/ApiGatewayE'; 214→ 215→export const useTransactionList = filters => { 216→ return useInfiniteQuery({ 217→ queryKey: [ApiGatewayE.BANK_TRANSACTIONS, filters], 218→ queryFn: async ({ pageParam = 1 }) => { 219→ return await requestClient.get(ApiGatewayE.BANK_TRANSACTIONS, undefined, { 220→ page: pageParam, 221→ ...filters, 222→ }); 223→ }, 224→ getNextPageParam: lastPage => { 225→ return lastPage.page < lastPage.totalPages ? lastPage.page + 1 : undefined; 226→ }, 227→ }); 228→}; 229→ 230→// In component - destructure what you need 231→const MyComponent = () => { 232→ const { data, isLoading, fetchNextPage, hasNextPage } = 233→ useTransactionList(filters); 234→ 235→ const transactions = data?.pages.flatMap(p => p.results) ?? []; 236→ 237→ return ; 238→}; 239→``` 240→ 241→### Mutation Hook Example (Write) 242→ 243→```typescript 244→import { useMutation } from '@tanstack/react-query'; 245→import { queryClient } from 'Shared/requestClient/queryClient'; 246→import { requestClient } from 'Shared/requestClient/requestClient'; 247→import { ApiGatewayE } from 'Shared/types/api/ApiGatewayE'; 248→ 249→export const useCreateTransaction = () => { 250→ const mutation = useMutation({ 251→ mutationFn: async data => { 252→ return await requestClient.post( 253→ ApiGatewayE.BANK_TRANSACTIONS, 254→ undefined, 255→ data, 256→ ); 257→ }, 258→ }); 259→ 260→ return async transactionBody => { 261→ await mutation.mutateAsync(transactionBody); 262→ 263→ // Invalidate cache to refetch 264→ await queryClient.invalidateQueries({ 265→ queryKey: [ApiGatewayE.BANK_TRANSACTIONS], 266→ }); 267→ }; 268→}; 269→ 270→// In component 271→const MyComponent = () => { 272→ const createTransaction = useCreateTransaction(); 273→ 274→ const handleCreate = async data => { 275→ try { 276→ await createTransaction(data); 277→ notificationService.addAlert('Transaction created!'); 278→ } catch (error) { 279→ notificationService.addErrorAlert(error.message); 280→ } 281→ }; 282→ 283→ return ; 284→}; 285→``` 286→ 287→### Example Files 288→ 289→- Query hook: `src/repositories/transactions/useTransactionList.ts` 290→- Mutation hook: `src/repositories/transactions/useCreateTransaction.ts` 291→- Request client: `src/shared/requestClient/requestClient.ts` 292→- Query client: `src/shared/requestClient/queryClient.ts` 293→- API endpoints: `src/shared/types/api/ApiGatewayE.ts` 294→ 295→--- 296→ 297→## 5. Form Components Pattern 298→ 299→### How It Works 300→ 301→Form components follow the `Au*` naming convention (Atomic UI). All form-related components are prefixed with `Au`. 302→ 303→**Available Components:** 304→ 305→- `AuButton` - Button component 306→- `AuField` - Form field wrapper (label + input + error) 307→- `AuDropdown` - Dropdown menu 308→- `AuSelect` - Select dropdown 309→- `AuCheckbox` - Checkbox input 310→- `AuRadioGroup` - Radio button group 311→- `AuDatePicker` - Date picker 312→- `AuForm` - Form container 313→- `AuFormBlock` - Form section wrapper 314→ 315→**Implementation:** 316→ 317→- Consistent API across all form components 318→- Built-in error handling and validation 319→- Accessible by default 320→- Styled with SCSS modules 321→ 322→### When to Use 323→ 324→- All form inputs and controls 325→- Interactive UI elements (buttons, toggles) 326→- Form layouts and sections 327→ 328→### Naming Rules 329→ 330→- **Always** prefix form components with `Au` 331→- Use PascalCase after prefix: `AuButton`, not `Aubutton` 332→- Place in `src/components/form/` directory 333→- Create subdirectory for each component: `form/button/AuButton.tsx` 334→ 335→### Usage Example 336→ 337→```typescript 338→import AuButton from 'Components/form/button/AuButton'; 339→import AuField from 'Components/form/field/AuField'; 340→import { AuButtonKindE } from 'Components/form/button/AuButtonKindE'; 341→import { validationPatterns } from 'Shared/constants/validationPatterns'; 342→import { FormProvider, useForm } from 'react-hook-form'; 343→ 344→const MyForm = () => { 345→ const formMethods = useForm({ 346→ defaultValues: { email: '', amount: '' }, 347→ }); 348→ 349→ const handleSubmit = async data => { 350→ // Handle form submission 351→ }; 352→ 353→ return ( 354→ 355→
356→ 357→ 358→ 359→ 360→ 366→ 367→ 368→ 369→ 370→ Save 371→ 372→
373→ 374→ ); 375→}; 376→``` 377→ 378→### Example Files 379→ 380→- Components: `src/components/form/*/Au*.tsx` 381→- Button: `src/components/form/button/AuButton.tsx` 382→- Field: `src/components/form/field/AuField.tsx` 383→- Dropdown: `src/components/form/dropdown/AuDropdown.tsx` 384→ 385→--- 386→ 387→## 6. Routing Pattern 388→ 389→### How It Works 390→ 391→Routing uses React Router v7 with Sentry integration. Routes are organized with route guards for authentication. 392→ 393→**Route Guards:** 394→ 395→- `OnlyPrivate` - Wraps authenticated routes, redirects to login if not authenticated 396→- `OnlyPublic` - Wraps public routes (login, registration), redirects to app if authenticated 397→ 398→**Implementation:** 399→ 400→- Routes defined in `AppRoutes.tsx` using `createRoutesFromElements` 401→- Sentry wraps router for error tracking 402→- Path constants defined in `appPaths` using `AppPath` class 403→- Nested route structure with guards as parent routes 404→ 405→### When to Use 406→ 407→- Adding new pages 408→- Protecting authenticated routes 409→- Defining navigation paths 410→ 411→### Usage Example 412→ 413→```typescript 414→// In appPaths.ts 415→export const appPaths = { 416→ TRANSACTIONS: new AppPath('/transactions'), 417→ BUDGET: new AppPath('/budgets/:budgetId'), 418→}; 419→ 420→// In AppRoutes.tsx 421→}> 422→ } /> 423→ } /> 424→ 425→ 426→}> 427→ } /> 428→ 429→ 430→// In component - navigate programmatically 431→import { useNavigate } from 'react-router-dom'; 432→import { appPaths } from 'Shared/constants/appPaths'; 433→ 434→const MyComponent = () => { 435→ const navigate = useNavigate(); 436→ 437→ const goToBudget = budgetId => { 438→ navigate(appPaths.BUDGET.getPath({ budgetId })); 439→ }; 440→ 441→ return ; 442→}; 443→``` 444→ 445→### Example Files 446→ 447→- Routes: `src/app/routes/AppRoutes.tsx` 448→- Route guards: `src/app/routes/OnlyPrivate.tsx`, `src/app/routes/OnlyPublic.tsx` 449→- Path constants: `src/shared/constants/appPaths.ts` 450→- Path helper: `src/shared/utils/url/AppPath.ts` 451→ 452→--- 453→ 454→## 7. Path Aliases 455→ 456→### How It Works 457→ 458→TypeScript path aliases provide absolute imports instead of relative paths. Configured in `tsconfig.json` and `vite.config.ts`. 459→ 460→**Available Aliases:** 461→ 462→- `Assets/*` → `src/assets/*` 463→- `Repositories/*` → `src/repositories/*` 464→- `Services/*` → `src/services/*` 465→- `Components/*` → `src/components/*` 466→- `Shared/*` → `src/shared/*` 467→ 468→**Important:** Both TypeScript and Vite configs must be kept in sync. 469→ 470→### When to Use 471→ 472→- **Always** use path aliases for imports 473→- **Never** use relative paths like `../../../` 474→- Makes refactoring easier 475→- Improves code readability 476→ 477→### Usage Example 478→ 479→```typescript 480→// ✅ GOOD - Use path aliases 481→import { sidebarService } from 'Services/sidebarService'; 482→import { useTransactionList } from 'Repositories/transactions/useTransactionList'; 483→import AuButton from 'Components/form/button/AuButton'; 484→import { TransactionDTO } from 'Shared/types/TransactionDTO'; 485→import logo from 'Assets/logo.svg'; 486→ 487→// ❌ BAD - Avoid relative paths 488→import { sidebarService } from '../../../services/sidebarService'; 489→import { useTransactionList } from '../../repositories/transactions/useTransactionList'; 490→import AuButton from '../../../components/form/button/AuButton'; 491→``` 492→ 493→### Configuration Files 494→ 495→- TypeScript config: `tsconfig.json` 496→- Vite config: `vite.config.ts` 497→ 498→--- 499→ 500→## 8. API Error Handling Pattern 501→ 502→### How It Works 503→ 504→API errors are handled globally through axios response interceptors. Different error types are handled differently: 505→ 506→1. **ValidationError (400)** - Form field validation errors shown inline 507→2. **AuthorizationError (401)** - Triggers logout, redirects to login 508→3. **Other API Errors** - Display in error modal with user-friendly message 509→ 510→**Implementation:** 511→ 512→- Global interceptor in `axiosClient.ts` catches all API errors 513→- `errorInterceptor.ts` handles displaying error modal 514→- ErrorModal component renders globally in App.tsx 515→- modalService manages error modal state 516→ 517→### When to Use 518→ 519→- All API errors that should notify the user 520→- Server errors (500) 521→- Network errors 522→- Any error not handled by ValidationError or AuthorizationError 523→ 524→### Usage Example 525→ 526→```typescript 527→// Automatic - errors are caught globally by axios interceptor 528→// No manual error handling needed for API errors 529→ 530→const handleSave = async () => { 531→ // If this fails with 500, error modal appears automatically 532→ await requestClient.post(ApiGatewayE.BUDGETS, undefined, budgetData); 533→ notificationService.addAlert('Budget saved!'); 534→}; 535→ 536→// Manual - if you need to show a specific error message 537→import { modalService } from 'Services/modalService'; 538→import { ModalNameE } from 'Components/modal/ModalNameE'; 539→ 540→modalService.open(ModalNameE.ERROR, { message: 'Custom error message' }); 541→``` 542→ 543→### Error Message Extraction 544→ 545→The interceptor extracts user-friendly messages in this order: 546→ 547→1. `response.data.message` (string) 548→2. `response.data.errors[0].msg` (validation format) 549→3. Fallback: `'Something went wrong. Please try again.'` 550→ 551→### Example Files 552→ 553→- Error interceptor: `src/shared/requestClient/errorInterceptor.ts` 554→- Axios client: `src/shared/requestClient/axiosClient.ts` 555→- Error modal component: `src/components/modal/errorModal/ErrorModal.tsx` 556→- Error modal styles: `src/components/modal/errorModal/errorModal.css` 557→ 558→--- 559→ 560→## 9. Theme System Pattern 561→ 562→### How It Works 563→ 564→The application automatically detects and applies theme colors based on the user's browser or operating system preference via the CSS media query `@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark)`. 565→ 566→**Implementation:** 567→ 568→- Theme variables defined in `src/app/styles/variables.scss` 569→- Light theme: Default CSS variables at `:root` 570→- Dark theme: Overridden variables in `@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) :root` 571→- Semantic color variables used throughout all components 572→- No manual theme toggle - theme switches automatically with OS/browser settings 573→ 574→### Semantic Color Variables 575→ 576→All components use semantic variables instead of specific colors. This ensures theme consistency. 577→ 578→**Light Theme Semantic Variables:** 579→ 580→```scss 581→--color-background: var(--color-primary-blue-light); 582→--color-foreground: #fff; 583→--color-text: #000; 584→--color-text-subtle: var(--color-gray-2); 585→--color-text-muted: var(--color-gray-1); 586→--color-border: var(--color-gray-4); 587→--color-border-subtle: var(--color-gray-3); 588→--color-placeholder: var(--color-gray-3); 589→--color-disabled-bg: var(--color-gray-4); 590→--color-disabled-text: var(--color-gray-3); 591→``` 592→ 593→**Dark Theme Semantic Variables:** 594→ 595→```scss 596→@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) { 597→ :root { 598→ --color-background: #0f1419; 599→ --color-foreground: #1a1f26; 600→ --color-text: #e0e0e0; 601→ --color-text-subtle: var(--color-gray-5); 602→ --color-text-muted: var(--color-gray-6); 603→ --color-border: var(--color-gray-7); 604→ --color-border-subtle: var(--color-gray-8); 605→ --color-placeholder: var(--color-gray-8); 606→ --color-disabled-bg: var(--color-gray-7); 607→ --color-disabled-text: var(--color-gray-6); 608→ } 609→} 610→``` 611→ 612→### Brand Color Adjustments 613→ 614→Brand blue colors are softened in dark mode for better contrast and readability: 615→ 616→**Light Mode:** 617→ 618→- `--color-primary-blue: #075eff` (bright, saturated blue) 619→- `--color-primary-blue-darker: #0640ac` 620→- `--color-primary-blue-light: #f3f7ff` 621→ 622→**Dark Mode:** 623→ 624→- `--color-primary-blue: #5a95ff` (softer, less saturated) 625→- `--color-primary-blue-darker: #7eabff` (lighter for contrast) 626→- `--color-primary-blue-light: #1a2a3d` (dark background instead of light) 627→ 628→### When to Use 629→ 630→- Use semantic variables for all color styling (never hardcoded colors) 631→- Use specific colors only for brand colors in variables.scss 632→- All SCSS files should reference semantic variables 633→ 634→### Usage Example 635→ 636→```scss 637→// SCSS file - use semantic variables only 638→.button { 639→ background-color: var(--color-button-primary); 640→ color: var(--color-button-text-on-primary); 641→ border: 1px solid var(--color-border); 642→ 643→ &:hover { 644→ background-color: var(--color-button-primary-hover); 645→ } 646→ 647→ &:disabled { 648→ background-color: var(--color-disabled-bg); 649→ color: var(--color-disabled-text); 650→ } 651→} 652→ 653→.text-subtle { 654→ color: var(--color-text-subtle); 655→} 656→ 657→.input { 658→ background-color: var(--color-foreground); 659→ color: var(--color-text); 660→ border: 1px solid var(--color-input-border); 661→ 662→ &::placeholder { 663→ color: var(--color-placeholder); 664→ } 665→} 666→``` 667→ 668→### Theme Switching 669→ 670→Theme switching happens automatically - no code needed: 671→ 672→1. User changes OS/browser theme preference 673→2. Browser applies matching `prefers-color-scheme` media query 674→3. CSS variables automatically update 675→4. All components re-render with new colors 676→ 677→**No manual theme toggle or localStorage required.** 678→ 679→### Example Files 680→ 681→- Variables: `src/app/styles/variables.scss` 682→- Helpers: `src/app/styles/helpers.scss` 683→- Component example: `src/components/form/button/auButton.scss` 684→ 685→--- 686→ 687→## Summary 688→ 689→### Key Principles 690→ 691→1. **Services manage UI state** - Use modalService, sidebarService for modals/sidebars 692→2. **Type-safe props** - Enums + mapped types ensure correct props at compile time 693→3. **Repository pattern** - All API calls go through repository hooks 694→4. **Absolute imports** - Always use path aliases 695→5. **Naming conventions** - Au* for forms, *Page for pages, \*Sidebar for sidebars 696→6. **Route guards** - Protect routes with OnlyPrivate/OnlyPublic 697→ 698→--- 699→ 700→## Common Workflows 701→ 702→### Opening a sidebar with form 703→ 704→```typescript 705→import { sidebarService } from 'Services/sidebarService'; 706→import { SidebarNameE } from 'Components/sidebar/types/SidebarNameE'; 707→ 708→// Clear stack and open 709→sidebarService.openFirst(SidebarNameE.CREATE_BUDGET, { budgetId }); 710→ 711→// Add to existing stack 712→sidebarService.open(SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET_ITEM, { itemId }); 713→``` 714→ 715→### Fetching and creating data 716→ 717→```typescript 718→import { useLoadBudgets } from 'Repositories/budgets/useLoadBudgets'; 719→import { useCreateBudget } from 'Repositories/budgets/useCreateBudget'; 720→ 721→const MyComponent = () => { 722→ const { data: budgets, isLoading } = useLoadBudgets(); 723→ const createBudget = useCreateBudget(); 724→ 725→ const handleCreate = async () => { 726→ await createBudget({ name: 'My Budget', startDate: new Date() }); 727→ }; 728→ 729→ return ( 730→ <> 731→ {isLoading && } 732→ {budgets?.map(budget => ( 733→ 734→ ))} 735→ New Budget 736→ 737→ ); 738→}; 739→``` 740→ 741→### Reading/updating global state 742→ 743→```typescript 744→import { useGlobalValue } from 'Shared/store/useGlobalValue'; 745→import { createGlobalValueSetter } from 'Shared/store/createGlobalValueSetter'; 746→import { GlobalStoreFieldE } from 'Shared/types/GlobalStoreFieldE'; 747→ 748→// In component (read) 749→const MyComponent = () => { 750→ const sidebars = useGlobalValue(GlobalStoreFieldE.SIDEBAR); 751→ return
{sidebars.length} sidebars open
; 752→}; 753→ 754→// In service (update) 755→const updateSidebar = createGlobalValueSetter(GlobalStoreFieldE.SIDEBAR); 756→ 757→export const sidebarService = { 758→ open: sidebar => { 759→ updateSidebar(current => [...current, sidebar]); 760→ }, 761→}; 762→``` 763→ 764→### Navigating to route 765→ 766→```typescript 767→import { useNavigate } from 'react-router-dom'; 768→import { appPaths } from 'Shared/constants/appPaths'; 769→ 770→const MyComponent = () => { 771→ const navigate = useNavigate(); 772→ 773→ const goToTransactions = () => { 774→ navigate(appPaths.TRANSACTIONS.getPath()); 775→ }; 776→ 777→ const goToBudget = budgetId => { 778→ navigate(appPaths.BUDGET.getPath({ budgetId })); 779→ }; 780→ 781→ return ( 782→ <> 783→ 784→ 785→ 786→ ); 787→}; 788→``` 789→ 790→### Form with validation 791→ 792→```typescript 793→import { FormProvider, useForm } from 'react-hook-form'; 794→import AuField from 'Components/form/field/AuField'; 795→import AuButton from 'Components/form/button/AuButton'; 796→import { validationPatterns } from 'Shared/constants/validationPatterns'; 797→import { replacePatterns } from 'Shared/constants/replacePatterns'; 798→ 799→const MyForm = () => { 800→ const formMethods = useForm(); 801→ 802→ const handleSubmit = async data => { 803→ try { 804→ await saveData(data); 805→ notificationService.addAlert('Saved!'); 806→ } catch (error) { 807→ notificationService.addErrorAlert(error.message); 808→ } 809→ }; 810→ 811→ return ( 812→ 813→
814→ 815→ 816→ 817→ 818→ 825→ 826→ 827→ 828→ Save 829→
830→ 831→ ); 832→}; 833→``` 834→ 835→--- 836→ 837→## For Agents 838→ 839→When implementing features: 840→ 841→1. **Check this doc first** - Find the relevant pattern 842→2. **Copy working examples** - Use code from this guide 843→3. **Follow naming conventions** - Consistency is key 844→4. **Use path aliases** - Never relative imports 845→5. **Test the pattern** - Ensure it works before moving on 846→ 847→If you need to understand _why_ a pattern exists, see [ARCHITECTURE.md](./ARCHITECTURE.md). 848→ Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. 1→# Project Structure

   101→
   102→## Services (`src/services/`)
   103→
   104→**See [SERVICES.md](./SERVICES.md) for detailed API documentation.**
   105→
   106→### Core Services
   107→

   126→## Pages (`src/pages/`)
   127→
   128→### Authentication
   129→

   131→
   132→### Transactions (`transactions/`)
   133→
   134→- `TransactionsPage` - Main page with list and filters
   135→- `TransactionForm` - Create/edit form

   139→
   140→### Bank Accounts (`bankAccounts/`)

   143→- `BankAccountForm` - Create/edit form

   171→
   172→## Repositories (`src/repositories/`)
   173→
   174→**Pattern:** Repository hooks wrap API calls with React Query.
   175→
   176→**Naming Convention:**
   177→
   178→- `useLoad*` - Fetch data (queries)
   179→- `useCreate*` - Create entities (mutations)
   180→- `useEdit*` - Update entities (mutations)
   181→- `useDelete*` - Delete entities (mutations)
   182→
   183→### Bank Accounts (`bankAccounts/`)
   184→
   185→- CRUD hooks: `useCreateBankAccount`, `useEditBankAccount`, `useDeleteBankAccount`
   186→- Query hooks: `useLoadBankAccount`, `useLoadBankAccountsList`
   187→- Types: `BankAccountDTO`, `CreateBankAccountSDTO`, `EditBankAccountSDTO`
   188→
   189→### Budgets (`budgets/`)
   190→
   191→- Budget CRUD: `useCreateBudget`, `useEditBudget`, `useDeleteBudget`, `useCloneBudget`
   192→- Budget queries: `useLoadBudget`, `useLoadBudgets`
   193→- Group CRUD: `useCreateBudgetGroup`, `useEditBudgetGroup`, `useDeleteBudgetGroup`
   194→- Group query: `useLoadBudgetGroups`
   195→- Item CRUD: `useCreateBudgetItem`, `useEditBudgetItem`, `useDeleteBudgetItem`
   196→- Item query: `useLoadBudgetItems`
   197→- Types: `BudgetDTO`, `BudgetSDTO`, `BudgetGroupDTO`, `BudgetItemDTO`
   198→
   199→### Categories (`category/`)
   200→
   201→- CRUD: `useCreateUserCategory`, `useEditUserCategory`, `useDeleteUserCategory`
   202→- Query: `useLoadUserCategoryList`, `useLoadUserBankAccountList`
   203→- Types: `UserCategoryDTO`, `UserCategorySDTO`
   204→
   205→### Transactions (`transactions/`)
   206→
   207→- CRUD: `useCreateTransaction`, `useEditTransaction`, `useDeleteTransactions` (bulk)
   208→- Queries: `useTransactionList`, `useOnLoadTransaction`, `useTransactionsFromCache`
   209→- Types: `TransactionDTO`, `CreateTransactionSDTO`, `EditTransactionSDTO`
   210→
   211→---
   212→
   213→## Types and Enums (`src/shared/types/`)
   214→
   215→### Core Enums
   216→
   217→- `CategoryTypeE` - INCOME, EXPENSE
   218→- `CurrencyE` - Supported currencies
   219→- `GlobalStoreFieldE` - Global store fields
   220→- `TransactionTypeE` - DEPOSIT, WITHDRAWAL
   221→- `TransactionSourceE` - Transaction sources
   222→- `SortOrderE` - ASC, DESC
   223→
   224→### API Types (`api/`)
   225→
   226→- `ApiGatewayE` - API endpoint definitions
   227→- `FormFieldValidationError` - Validation errors
   228→- `RequestListDataT` - Paginated list response
   229→- `RequestRecordDataT` - Single record response
   230→
   231→### Other Types
   232→
   233→- `UuidT` - UUID type
   234→- `NotificationT` - Notification object
   235→- `ValidationPatternT` - Validation patterns
   236→- `ReplacePatternT` - Text replacement patterns
   237→
   238→---
   239→
   240→## Path Aliases
   241→
   242→**See [PATTERNS.md](./PATTERNS.md#7-path-aliases) for usage examples.**
   243→
   244→Configured in `tsconfig.json` and `vite.config.ts`:
   245→
   246→- `Assets/*` → `src/assets/*`
   247→- `Components/*` → `src/components/*`
   248→- `Services/*` → `src/services/*`
   249→- `Repositories/*` → `src/repositories/*`
   250→- `Shared/*` → `src/shared/*`
   251→
   252→**Always use path aliases instead of relative imports.**
   253→
   254→---
   255→
   256→## Key Application Features
   257→
   258→### 1. Authentication
   259→
   260→Email-based authentication with 6-digit confirmation codes. See [BUSINESS_RULES.md](./BUSINESS_RULES.md#authentication-rules).
   261→
   262→### 2. Transaction Management
   263→
   264→Create, edit, delete transactions with filtering and bulk operations. See [BUSINESS_RULES.md](./BUSINESS_RULES.md#transaction-rules).
   265→
   266→### 3. Bank Accounts
   267→
   268→Manage multiple bank accounts with different types and sources.
   269→
   270→### 4. Budgeting System
   271→
   272→Create budgets with groups and items. Drag-and-drop reordering. Track spending against budget. See [BUSINESS_RULES.md](./BUSINESS_RULES.md#budget-rules).
   273→
   274→### 5. Category Management
   275→
   276→Create custom income/expense categories. See [BUSINESS_RULES.md](./BUSINESS_RULES.md#category-rules).
   277→
   278→### 6. AI Assistant
   279→
   280→Chat-based budget assistant with conversation history. See [SERVICES.md](./SERVICES.md#ai-assistant-services).
   281→
   282→### 7. State Management
   283→
   284→- Global state: @preact/signals-react. See [ARCHITECTURE.md](./ARCHITECTURE.md#state-management-architecture).
   285→- Server state: @tanstack/react-query. See [PATTERNS.md](./PATTERNS.md#4-data-fetching-pattern).
   286→- Form state: react-hook-form. See [ARCHITECTURE.md](./ARCHITECTURE.md#form-state-react-hook-form).
   287→
   288→### 8. UI Patterns
   289→
   290→- Service-managed modals and sidebars. See [PATTERNS.md](./PATTERNS.md#1-modal-pattern).
   291→- Toast notifications. See [SERVICES.md](./SERVICES.md#notificationservice).
   292→- Loading states and empty states.
   293→
   294→---
   295→
   296→## Development Commands
   297→
   298→```bash
   299→yarn dev          # Start dev server
   300→yarn build        # Production build
   301→yarn lint         # Run ESLint
   302→yarn lint:fix     # Fix ESLint issues
   303→yarn test         # Run unit tests
   304→yarn test:watch   # Run tests in watch mode
   305→yarn test:e2e     # Run E2E tests
   306→```
   307→
   308→---
   309→
   310→## Testing Structure
   311→
   312→- **Unit tests:** Adjacent to source files (`Component.test.tsx`)
   313→- **E2E tests:** `tests/` directory (Playwright)
   314→- **Test coverage:** Focus on services, utils, business logic, complex components
   315→
   316→See [ARCHITECTURE.md](./ARCHITECTURE.md#testing-architecture) for testing strategy.
   317→
   318→---
   319→
   320→## For Agents
   321→
   322→When working on tasks:
   323→
   324→1. **Check PROJECT_STRUCTURE.md** - Find what exists and where
   325→2. **Check PATTERNS.md** - Learn how to implement features
   326→3. **Check COMPONENTS.md/SERVICES.md** - API reference for existing code
   327→4. **Check BUSINESS_RULES.md** - Understand validation and business logic
   328→5. **Check ARCHITECTURE.md** - Understand technical decisions
   329→
   330→Start here, then dive into specific documentation as needed.
   331→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

# Project Structure

**Purpose:** Quick reference index of what exists in the codebase and where to find detailed documentation.

---

## Documentation Map

- **[ARCHITECTURE.md](./ARCHITECTURE.md)** - Technical decisions and why specific technologies were chosen
- **[PATTERNS.md](./PATTERNS.md)** - How to use established patterns with practical examples
- **[COMPONENTS.md](./COMPONENTS.md)** - Detailed component API reference with props and usage
- **[SERVICES.md](./SERVICES.md)** - Detailed service API reference with methods and examples
- **[BUSINESS_RULES.md](./BUSINESS_RULES.md)** - Business logic, validation rules, and calculations

---

## Directory Structure

```
client/
├── src/
│   ├── components/          # React components
│   │   ├── form/           # Form components (Au* prefix)
│   │   ├── layouts/        # Layout components
│   │   ├── modal/          # Modal system
│   │   ├── sidebar/        # Sidebar system
│   │   └── svg/            # SVG icon components
│   ├── pages/              # Page components (*Page suffix)
│   │   ├── auth/           # Authentication pages
│   │   ├── transactions/   # Transaction management
│   │   ├── bankAccounts/   # Bank account management
│   │   ├── budgets/        # Budget listing
│   │   ├── budget/         # Single budget view
│   │   └── userCategory/   # Category management
│   ├── services/           # Business logic services
│   ├── repositories/       # Data fetching hooks (use* prefix)
│   ├── shared/             # Shared utilities and types
│   │   ├── store/          # Global state (signals)
│   │   ├── types/          # TypeScript types and enums
│   │   ├── constants/      # App constants
│   │   └── utils/          # Utility functions
│   └── app/                # App initialization and routing
├── tests/                  # E2E tests (Playwright)
├── .claude/                # Multi-agent system configuration
│   ├── docs/              # Documentation for agents
│   ├── agents/            # Agent-specific instructions
│   └── scripts/           # Helper scripts
└── tasks/                 # Agent task outputs
```

---

## Components (`src/components/`)

**See [COMPONENTS.md](./COMPONENTS.md) for detailed API documentation.**

### Form Components (`form/`)

- `AuButton` - Button component with variants and sizes
- `AuField` - Form field wrapper with validation
- `AuSelect` - Select dropdown
- `AuCheckbox` - Checkbox input
- `AuRadioGroup` - Radio button group
- `AuDatePicker` - Date picker
- `AuDropdown` - Dropdown menu
- `AuForm` - Form container
- `AuFormBlock` - Form section wrapper

### Layout Components (`layouts/`)

- `MainLayout` - Main application layout
- `BaseLayout` - Base layout wrapper
- `Menu` - Navigation menu
- `Navigation` - Navigation links
- `LogoutButton` - Logout button

### Modal System (`modal/`)

- `Modal` - Modal dialog component

### Sidebar System (`sidebar/`)

- `Sidebar` - Sidebar container with animations
- `SidebarLayout` - Sidebar layout wrapper

### UI Components

- `PageHeader` - Page header with title and actions
- `Spinner` - Loading spinner
- `EmptyState` - Empty state placeholder
- `CategoryBadge` - Category badge display
- `EditableLabel` - Inline editable label
- `ActionDropdown` - Dropdown menu for actions
- `AIAssistant` - AI chat assistant

### SVG Icons (`svg/`)

- Icon components: `BackSvg`, `CalendarSvg`, `ClearSvg`, `CloseSvg`, `CopySvg`, `EditSvg`, `FileImportSvg`, `FilterSvg`, `HamburgerSvg`, `LogoutSvg`, `PencilSvg`, `PlusSvg`, `TrashSvg`, and more

---

## Services (`src/services/`)

**See [SERVICES.md](./SERVICES.md) for detailed API documentation.**

### Core Services

- `authService` - Authentication and token management
- `modalService` - Modal state management
- `sidebarService` - Sidebar state management with stacking
- `notificationService` - Toast notifications
- `scrollLockService` - Body scroll prevention

### AI Services

- `aiAssistantService` - AI chat backend communication
- `aiConversationStorageService` - AI conversation persistence

### Storage Services

- `localStorageService` - Type-safe localStorage wrapper
- `cookieService` - Type-safe cookie management

---

## Pages (`src/pages/`)

### Authentication

- `AuthPage`, `LoginPage`, `LogoutPage`, `RegistrationPage`, `ProfilePage`

### Transactions (`transactions/`)

- `TransactionsPage` - Main page with list and filters
- `TransactionForm` - Create/edit form
- `TransactionFilter` - Filter component
- `TransactionList` - List display
- Sidebars: Form, Edit, Info, Delete, Bulk, AddCategories

### Bank Accounts (`bankAccounts/`)

- `BankAccountsPage` - Main page
- `BankAccountForm` - Create/edit form
- `BankAccountsList` - List display
- Sidebars: Create, Edit, Info, Delete

### Budgets (`budgets/`)

- `BudgetsPage` - Budget listing
- `BudgetForm` - Create/edit form
- `BudgetPreview` - Budget card
- Sidebars: Create, Edit, Clone, Delete

### Budget (`budget/`)

- `BudgetPage` - Single budget with groups and items
- `BudgetView` - Overview display
- `BudgetInsights` - Analytics
- `BudgetGroup` - Group container (draggable)
- `BudgetItem` - Item display (draggable)
- Group Sidebars: Create, Edit, Delete
- Item Sidebars: Create, Edit, Delete

### User Categories (`userCategory/`)

- `UserCategoryPage` - Category management
- `CategoryForm` - Create/edit form
- Sidebars: Create, Edit, Delete

---

## Repositories (`src/repositories/`)

**Pattern:** Repository hooks wrap API calls with React Query.

**Naming Convention:**

- `useLoad*` - Fetch data (queries)
- `useCreate*` - Create entities (mutations)
- `useEdit*` - Update entities (mutations)
- `useDelete*` - Delete entities (mutations)

### Bank Accounts (`bankAccounts/`)

- CRUD hooks: `useCreateBankAccount`, `useEditBankAccount`, `useDeleteBankAccount`
- Query hooks: `useLoadBankAccount`, `useLoadBankAccountsList`
- Types: `BankAccountDTO`, `CreateBankAccountSDTO`, `EditBankAccountSDTO`

### Budgets (`budgets/`)

- Budget CRUD: `useCreateBudget`, `useEditBudget`, `useDeleteBudget`, `useCloneBudget`
- Budget queries: `useLoadBudget`, `useLoadBudgets`
- Group CRUD: `useCreateBudgetGroup`, `useEditBudgetGroup`, `useDeleteBudgetGroup`
- Group query: `useLoadBudgetGroups`
- Item CRUD: `useCreateBudgetItem`, `useEditBudgetItem`, `useDeleteBudgetItem`
- Item query: `useLoadBudgetItems`
- Types: `BudgetDTO`, `BudgetSDTO`, `BudgetGroupDTO`, `BudgetItemDTO`

### Categories (`category/`)

- CRUD: `useCreateUserCategory`, `useEditUserCategory`, `useDeleteUserCategory`
- Query: `useLoadUserCategoryList`, `useLoadUserBankAccountList`
- Types: `UserCategoryDTO`, `UserCategorySDTO`

### Transactions (`transactions/`)

- CRUD: `useCreateTransaction`, `useEditTransaction`, `useDeleteTransactions` (bulk)
- Queries: `useTransactionList`, `useOnLoadTransaction`, `useTransactionsFromCache`
- Types: `TransactionDTO`, `CreateTransactionSDTO`, `EditTransactionSDTO`

---

## Types and Enums (`src/shared/types/`)

### Core Enums

- `CategoryTypeE` - INCOME, EXPENSE
- `CurrencyE` - Supported currencies
- `GlobalStoreFieldE` - Global store fields
- `TransactionTypeE` - DEPOSIT, WITHDRAWAL
- `TransactionSourceE` - Transaction sources
- `SortOrderE` - ASC, DESC

### API Types (`api/`)

- `ApiGatewayE` - API endpoint definitions
- `FormFieldValidationError` - Validation errors
- `RequestListDataT` - Paginated list response
- `RequestRecordDataT` - Single record response

### Other Types

- `UuidT` - UUID type
- `NotificationT` - Notification object
- `ValidationPatternT` - Validation patterns
- `ReplacePatternT` - Text replacement patterns

---

## Path Aliases

**See [PATTERNS.md](./PATTERNS.md#7-path-aliases) for usage examples.**

Configured in `tsconfig.json` and `vite.config.ts`:

- `Assets/*` → `src/assets/*`
- `Components/*` → `src/components/*`
- `Services/*` → `src/services/*`
- `Repositories/*` → `src/repositories/*`
- `Shared/*` → `src/shared/*`

**Always use path aliases instead of relative imports.**

---

## Key Application Features

### 1. Authentication

Email-based authentication with 6-digit confirmation codes. See [BUSINESS_RULES.md](./BUSINESS_RULES.md#authentication-rules).

### 2. Transaction Management

Create, edit, delete transactions with filtering and bulk operations. See [BUSINESS_RULES.md](./BUSINESS_RULES.md#transaction-rules).

### 3. Bank Accounts

Manage multiple bank accounts with different types and sources.

### 4. Budgeting System

Create budgets with groups and items. Drag-and-drop reordering. Track spending against budget. See [BUSINESS_RULES.md](./BUSINESS_RULES.md#budget-rules).

### 5. Category Management

Create custom income/expense categories. See [BUSINESS_RULES.md](./BUSINESS_RULES.md#category-rules).

### 6. AI Assistant

Chat-based budget assistant with conversation history. See [SERVICES.md](./SERVICES.md#ai-assistant-services).

### 7. State Management

- Global state: @preact/signals-react. See [ARCHITECTURE.md](./ARCHITECTURE.md#state-management-architecture).
- Server state: @tanstack/react-query. See [PATTERNS.md](./PATTERNS.md#4-data-fetching-pattern).
- Form state: react-hook-form. See [ARCHITECTURE.md](./ARCHITECTURE.md#form-state-react-hook-form).

### 8. UI Patterns

- Service-managed modals and sidebars. See [PATTERNS.md](./PATTERNS.md#1-modal-pattern).
- Toast notifications. See [SERVICES.md](./SERVICES.md#notificationservice).
- Loading states and empty states.

---

## Development Commands

```bash
yarn dev          # Start dev server
yarn build        # Production build
yarn lint         # Run ESLint
yarn lint:fix     # Fix ESLint issues
yarn test         # Run unit tests
yarn test:watch   # Run tests in watch mode
yarn test:e2e     # Run E2E tests
```

---

## Testing Structure

- **Unit tests:** Adjacent to source files (`Component.test.tsx`)
- **E2E tests:** `tests/` directory (Playwright)
- **Test coverage:** Focus on services, utils, business logic, complex components

See [ARCHITECTURE.md](./ARCHITECTURE.md#testing-architecture) for testing strategy.

---

## For Agents

When working on tasks:

1. **Check PROJECT_STRUCTURE.md** - Find what exists and where
2. **Check PATTERNS.md** - Learn how to implement features
3. **Check COMPONENTS.md/SERVICES.md** - API reference for existing code
4. **Check BUSINESS_RULES.md** - Understand validation and business logic
5. **Check ARCHITECTURE.md** - Understand technical decisions

Start here, then dive into specific documentation as needed.

21→``` 22→ 23→**Behavior:** 24→ 25→- Reads `ACCESS_TOKEN` from localStorage 26→- If token exists, updates global auth state 27→- Sets Sentry user from token for error tracking 28→ 29→**When to call:** App initialization (in root component or App.tsx) 30→ 31→--- 32→ 33→#### `login(token: string)` 34→ 35→Stores auth token and updates global state after successful login. 36→ 37→```typescript 38→authService.login('eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9...'); 57→``` 58→ 59→**Behavior:** 60→ 61→- Removes token from localStorage 62→- Clears global `AUTH_TOKEN` state 63→- Clears all React Query cache 64→- Clears Sentry user data 65→ 66→**When to call:** User logout, session expiry, or unauthorized errors 67→ 68→--- 69→ 70→**Dependencies:** 71→ 72→- `localStorageService` - Token persistence 73→- `globalStore` - Auth state management 74→- `queryClient` - Cache clearing 75→- `sentryHelpers` - Error tracking 76→ 77→**Usage Example:** 78→ 79→```typescript 80→// On app start 81→useEffect(() => { 82→ authService.initialize(); // ✅ 273→sidebarService.open(SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET); // ❌ Missing required props 274→``` 275→ 276→**Dependencies:** `globalStore`, `createGlobalValueSetter` 277→ 278→--- 279→ 280→### notificationService 281→ 282→**Location:** `src/services/notificationService.ts` 283→ 284→**Purpose:** Manages toast notifications with auto-dismiss timers. 285→ 286→**Methods:** 287→ 288→#### `addAlert(content: ReactNode, showTime?: number)` 289→ 290→Adds an info notification. 291→ 292→```typescript 293→notificationService.addAlert('Budget saved successfully!'); Uses reference counting to support multiple locks. 407→ 408→**Methods:** 409→ 410→#### `scrollLockManager.add(source: string)` 411→ 412→Adds a scroll lock from a specific source. 413→ 414→```typescript 415→scrollLockManager.add('my-sidebar'); 416→``` 417→ 418→**Behavior:** 419→ 420→- Adds source to internal Set 421→- If first lock, adds `modal-open` class to `` 422→- `modal-open` class applies `overflow: hidden` via CSS 423→ 424→--- 425→ 426→#### `scrollLockManager.remove(source: string)` 427→ 428→Removes a scroll lock from a specific source. 429→ 430→```typescript 431→scrollLockManager.remove('my-sidebar'); 432→``` 433→ 434→**Behavior:** 435→ 436→- Removes source from internal Set 437→- If last lock removed, removes `modal-open` class from `` 438→- Body scroll is restored 439→ 440→--- 441→ 442→**How It Works:** 443→ 444→```typescript 445→// Multiple sources can lock scroll 446→scrollLockManager.add('modal-1'); // Body locked 447→scrollLockManager.add('modal-2'); // Still locked 448→scrollLockManager.remove('modal-1'); // Still locked (modal-2 active) 449→scrollLockManager.remove('modal-2'); // Body unlocked 450→``` 451→ 452→**Usage Example:** 453→ 454→```typescript 455→// In Sidebar component 456→useEffect(() => { 457→ if (isOpen) { 458→ scrollLockManager.add('my-sidebar'); 459→ 460→ return () => { 461→ scrollLockManager.remove('my-sidebar'); 462→ }; 463→ } 464→}, [isOpen]); 465→``` 466→ 467→**Note:** Sidebar component automatically manages scroll lock. 617→``` 618→ 619→**Behavior:** 620→ 621→- Removes conversation from localStorage 622→- Catches and logs errors silently 623→ 624→--- 625→ 626→**Message Format:** 627→ 628→```typescript 629→type AIMessageT = { 630→ id: string; 631→ role: AIRoleE; // 'user' | 'assistant' 632→ content: string; 633→ timestamp: Date; 634→}; 635→``` 636→ 637→**Usage Example:** 638→ 639→```typescript 640→// Save after each message 641→const handleSendMessage = async (text: string) => { 642→ const newMessage: AIMessageT = { 643→ id: generateId(), 644→ role: AIRoleE.USER, 645→ content: text, 646→ timestamp: new Date(), 647→ }; 686→``` 687→ 688→**Behavior:** 689→ 690→- Serializes value to JSON 691→- Stores in localStorage 692→- Ignores `undefined` and `null` values 693→ 694→--- 695→ 696→#### `get(name: T)` 697→ 698→Retrieves value from localStorage. 699→ 700→```typescript 701→const token = localStorageService.get(LocalStorageNameE.ACCESS_TOKEN); 702→``` 703→ 704→**Returns:** `LocalStorageT[T] | null` 705→ 706→**Behavior:** 707→ 708→- Parses JSON from localStorage 709→- Returns `null` if not found 710→- On parse error, removes corrupted item and returns `null` 711→ 712→--- 713→ 714→#### `remove(name: T)` 715→ 716→Removes value from localStorage. 717→ 718→```typescript 719→localStorageService.remove(LocalStorageNameE.ACCESS_TOKEN); 720→``` 721→ 722→--- 723→ 724→**Type Safety:** 725→ 726→```typescript 727→// LocalStorageNameE defines keys 728→export enum LocalStorageNameE { 729→ ACCESS_TOKEN = 'accessToken', 730→ AI_CONVERSATION_STORAGE = 'aiConversationStorage', 731→} 732→ 733→// LocalStorageT maps keys to value types 734→export type LocalStorageT = { 735→ [LocalStorageNameE.ACCESS_TOKEN]: string; 736→ [LocalStorageNameE.AI_CONVERSATION_STORAGE]: AIMessageStorageT[]; 737→}; 738→ 739→// TypeScript ensures correct types 740→const token: string | null = localStorageService.get( 741→ LocalStorageNameE.ACCESS_TOKEN, 742→); 743→``` 744→ 745→**Usage Example:** 746→ 747→```typescript 748→// Store user preference 749→localStorageService.set(LocalStorageNameE.THEME, 'dark'); 750→ 751→// Retrieve on app start 752→const theme = localStorageService.get(LocalStorageNameE.THEME) || 'light'; 753→ 754→// Clear on logout 755→localStorageService.remove(LocalStorageNameE.USER_PREFERENCES); 756→``` 757→ 758→**Dependencies:** `jsonHelpers` (custom JSON parse/stringify with error handling) 759→ 760→--- 761→ 762→### cookieService 763→ 764→**Location:** `src/services/cookieService.ts` 765→ 766→**Purpose:** Type-safe wrapper for browser cookies using `universal-cookie` library. 767→ 768→**Methods:** 769→ 770→#### `set(name: T, value: CookieT[T], days?: number)` 771→ 772→Stores value in cookie with expiration. 773→ 774→```typescript 775→cookieService.set(CookieNameE.USER_ID, 'user-123', 30); 776→``` 777→ 778→**Parameters:** 779→ 780→- `name` - Cookie name from `CookieNameE` 781→- `value` - Cookie value (type-safe) 782→- `days` - Expiration in days (default: 180) 783→ 784→**Behavior:** 785→ 786→- Sets cookie with expiration date 787→- Path is always `/` (available site-wide) 788→ 789→--- 790→ 791→#### `get(name: T)` 792→ 793→Retrieves cookie value. 794→ 795→```typescript 796→const userId = cookieService.get(CookieNameE.USER_ID); 797→``` 798→ 799→**Returns:** `CookieT[T] | undefined` 800→ 801→--- 802→ 803→#### `remove(name: T)` 804→ 805→Removes cookie. 806→ 807→```typescript 808→cookieService.remove(CookieNameE.USER_ID); 809→``` 810→ 811→--- 812→ 813→**Type Safety:** 814→ 815→```typescript 816→// CookieNameE defines cookie names 817→export enum CookieNameE { 818→ USER_ID = 'userId', 819→ PREFERENCES = 'preferences', 820→} 821→ 822→// CookieT maps names to value types 823→export type CookieT = { 824→ [CookieNameE.USER_ID]: string; 825→ [CookieNameE.PREFERENCES]: PreferencesT; 826→}; 827→``` 828→ 829→**Usage Example:** 830→ 831→```typescript 832→// Remember user preference 833→cookieService.set(CookieNameE.LANGUAGE, 'en', 365); 834→ 835→// Read on page load 836→const language = cookieService.get(CookieNameE.LANGUAGE) || 'en'; 837→ 838→// Clear on logout 839→cookieService.remove(CookieNameE.SESSION_ID); 840→``` 841→ 842→**Dependencies:** `universal-cookie` 843→ 844→--- 845→ 846→## Service Patterns 847→ 848→### Pattern 1: Global State Services 849→ 850→Services that manage global state using signals. 851→ 852→**Services:** `authService`, `modalService`, `sidebarService`, `notificationService` 853→ 854→**Pattern:** 855→ 856→```typescript 857→import { createGlobalValueSetter } from 'Shared/store/createGlobalValueSetter'; 858→import { GlobalStoreFieldE } from 'Shared/types/GlobalStoreFieldE'; 859→ 860→const updateField = createGlobalValueSetter(GlobalStoreFieldE.MY_FIELD); 861→ 862→export const myService = { 863→ add: item => { 864→ updateField(current => [...current, item]); 865→ }, 866→ remove: id => { 867→ updateField(current => current.filter(i => i.id !== id)); 868→ }, 869→}; 870→``` 871→ 872→**Why:** 873→ 874→- Centralized state management 875→- Type-safe updates 876→- Reactive - components re-render automatically 877→- Service logic separated from UI 878→ 879→--- 880→ 881→### Pattern 2: Storage Services 882→ 883→Services that wrap browser storage APIs with type safety. 884→ 885→**Services:** `localStorageService`, `cookieService` 886→ 887→**Pattern:** 888→ 889→```typescript 890→export const storageService = { 891→ set(name: T, value: TypeMap[T]) { 892→ // Type-safe set 893→ }, 894→ get(name: T): TypeMap[T] | null { 895→ // Type-safe get with return type inference 896→ }, 897→ remove(name: T) { 898→ // Type-safe remove 899→ }, 900→}; 901→``` 902→ 903→**Benefits:** 904→ 905→- TypeScript ensures correct types for each key 906→- Centralized storage logic 907→- Easy to add new storage keys 908→- Consistent error handling 909→ 910→--- 911→ 912→### Pattern 3: API Services 913→ 914→Services that wrap API calls with error handling. 915→ 916→**Services:** `aiAssistantService` 917→ 918→**Pattern:** 919→ 920→```typescript 921→export const apiService = { 922→ async operation(params): Promise { 923→ try { 924→ const response = await axiosClient.post(endpoint, params); 925→ return response.data; 926→ } catch (error) { 927→ if (error instanceof AxiosError) { 928→ // Handle specific errors 929→ if (error.code === 'ECONNABORTED') { 930→ throw new Error('User-friendly message'); 931→ } 932→ } 933→ throw error; // Re-throw unknown errors 934→ } 935→ }, 936→}; 937→``` 938→ 939→**Benefits:** 940→ 941→- Consistent error handling 942→- User-friendly error messages 943→- Timeout handling 944→- Type-safe responses 945→ 946→--- 947→ 948→## Error Handling 949→ 950→### General Principles 951→ 952→1. **Catch at service level** - Services should catch and transform errors 953→2. **User-friendly messages** - Convert technical errors to readable messages 954→3. **Log for debugging** - Console errors in development 955→4. **Graceful degradation** - Return sensible defaults when possible 956→ 957→### Examples 958→ 959→**Storage Services:** 960→ 961→```typescript 962→try { 963→ return JSON.parse(data); 964→} catch { 965→ // Remove corrupted data and return null 966→ localStorage.removeItem(key); 967→ return null; 968→} 969→``` 970→ 971→**API Services:** 972→ 973→```typescript 974→catch (error: unknown) { 975→ if (error instanceof AxiosError) { 976→ if (error.code === 'ECONNABORTED') { 977→ throw new Error('Request timed out'); 978→ } 979→ if (error.response?.status === 500) { 980→ throw new Error('Server error'); 981→ } 982→ } 983→ throw error; // Unknown errors re-thrown 984→} 985→``` 986→ 987→**Notification:** 988→ 989→```typescript 990→try { 991→ await riskyOperation(); 992→ notificationService.addAlert('Success!'); 993→} catch (error) { 994→ notificationService.addErrorAlert(error.message); 995→} 996→``` 997→ 998→--- 999→ 1000→## Testing Services 1001→ 1002→Services should have tests in `__tests__` directories: 1003→ 1004→``` 1005→src/services/ 1006→ ├── authService.ts 1007→ ├── authService.test.ts 1008→ ├── modalService.ts 1009→ └── modalService.test.ts 1010→``` 1011→ 1012→**Test Guidelines:** 1013→ 1014→- Test each public method 1015→- Test error cases 1016→- Mock dependencies (storage, API calls) 1017→- Test state changes for state-management services 1018→ 1019→**Example:** 1020→ 1021→```typescript 1022→describe('authService', () => { 1023→ it('should initialize with token from localStorage', () => { 1024→ localStorageService.set(LocalStorageNameE.ACCESS_TOKEN, 'token-123'); 1025→ 1026→ authService.initialize(); 1027→ 1028→ expect(globalStore[GlobalStoreFieldE.AUTH_TOKEN].value).toBe('token-123'); 1029→ }); 1030→ 1031→ it('should clear cache on logout', () => { 1032→ authService.logout(); 1033→ 1034→ expect(queryClient.clear).toHaveBeenCalled(); 1035→ }); 1036→}); 1037→``` 1038→ 1039→--- 1040→ 1041→## Request Client Utilities 1042→ 1043→### errorInterceptor 1044→ 1045→**Location:** `src/shared/requestClient/errorInterceptor.ts` 1046→ 1047→**Purpose:** Global API error handling via axios interceptor. Displays user-friendly error messages in a modal for all API errors not handled by ValidationError or AuthorizationError. 1048→ 1049→**Exports:** 1050→ 1051→#### `handleErrorModal(error: AxiosError)` 1052→ 1053→Opens an error modal with a user-friendly message extracted from the API response. 1054→ 1055→```typescript 1056→import { handleErrorModal } from 'Shared/requestClient/errorInterceptor'; 1057→ 1058→// Called automatically by axios interceptor 1059→handleErrorModal(error); 1060→``` 1061→ 1062→**Message Extraction Logic:** 1063→ 1064→1. First tries `response.data.message` (string) 1065→2. Falls back to `response.data.errors[0].msg` (validation error format) 1066→3. Falls back to `'Something went wrong. Please try again.'` 1067→ 1068→**Integration:** 1069→ 1070→The interceptor is integrated in `axiosClient.ts` after ValidationError (400) and AuthorizationError (401) handlers: 1071→ 1072→```typescript 1073→// In axiosClient.ts response interceptor 1074→axiosClient.interceptors.response.use( 1075→ response => response, 1076→ error => { 1077→ // Existing ValidationError handling (400 with errors array) 1078→ // Existing AuthorizationError handling (401) 1079→ 1080→ // NEW: Handle all other API errors with modal 1081→ handleErrorModal(error); 1082→ 1083→ return Promise.reject(error); 1084→ }, 1085→); 1086→``` 1087→ 1088→**Errors NOT Handled:** 1089→ 1090→- ValidationError (400 with validation errors) - handled by form fields 1091→- AuthorizationError (401) - triggers logout, no modal 1092→- Network errors during critical auth operations 1093→ 1094→**Dependencies:** `modalService`, `ModalNameE` 1095→ 1096→**Tests:** 1097→ 1098→- Unit: `errorInterceptor.test.ts` (9 tests) 1099→ 1100→--- 1101→ 1102→## Service Dependencies 1103→ 1104→``` 1105→authService 1106→ ├── localStorageService 1107→ ├── globalStore 1108→ ├── queryClient 1109→ └── sentryHelpers 1110→ 1111→modalService 1112→ └── globalStore 1113→ 1114→sidebarService 1115→ └── globalStore 1116→ 1117→notificationService 1118→ └── globalStore 1119→ 1120→scrollLockService 1121→ └── (none - pure DOM) 1122→ 1123→aiAssistantService 1124→ └── axiosClient 1125→ 1126→aiConversationStorageService 1127→ └── localStorageService 1128→ 1129→localStorageService 1130→ └── jsonHelpers 1131→ 1132→cookieService 1133→ └── universal-cookie (external) 1134→``` 1135→ Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

1→# Architecture Documentation

     7→## Technology Stack

    21→### Routing

    29→
    30→### UI & Styling

    39→### Testing
